location: [1] - 1 Found on a corpse in the Royal Forest. [2] - Gift x3 from Elizabeth, after rescuing Dusk from Manus. Also drops from her if you kill Elizabeth.

description: The Undead treasure these dull green flasks. Fill with Estus at Bonfire. Fills HP. The Estus Flasks are linked to the Fire Keepers. The Dark Tales also make reference: An emerald flask, from the Keeper's soul. She lives to protect the flame, and dies to protect it further
location: [1] - Video Location Timestamp 1:52.

description: Soul of a long-lost Fire Keeper. Each Fire Keeper is a corporeal manifestation of her bonfire, and a draw for the humanity which is offered to her. Her soul is gnawed by infinite humanity, and can boost the power of the precious Estus Flasks. It can be used to gain Humanity and restore HP at the cost of losing the Fire Keeper soul to reinforce the Estus Flasks.
location: [1] - One is located in Undead Parish. It is on an altar in the Undead Church (2nd section of Undead Parish), guarded by a Berenike Knight. [2] - One is located in New Londo Ruins. It can be seen on the right hand when crossing the narrow wooden walkway. Three ghosts will spawn when getting near. [3] - One is located in Blighttown. In a wastewater pit, about halfway down from the Valley of Drakes entrance, guarded by fire-dogs and toxic dart shooters. [4] - One is located in the Duke's Archives. At the bottom of the prison tower, behind the pisaca. You need the Archive Tower Giant Cell Key that is acquired later in the level to access it (don't confuse this with the Archive Tower Giant Door Key). [5] - One is obtained after killing Quelaag's Sister. Please note that you won't be able to join the Chaos Servant covenant or use the bonfire in front of her anymore (with the exclusion of being able to warp to, but not from, that location). [6] - One is obtained after killing the Darkmoon Knightess fire keeper in Anor Londo. If you decide to kill her for her soul, her bonfire will no longer be lit but you may still warp there using the Lordvessel. [7] - One can be obtained if you allow Knight Lautrec to kill Anastacia of Astora, then later kill Knight Lautrec in Anor Londo using the Black Eye Orb that was found at Anastacia's dead body (under the Firelink Shrine bonfire.) This can be used to upgrade your flask but it may also be a viable option to return the soul to the Anastacia's cell under the bonfire. [8] - Video Location Timestamp 5:12. [9] - Video Location Timestamp 15:55. [10] - Video Location Timestamp 28:32. [11] - Video Location Timestamp 1:54.

description: Rare pine resin which emits golden sparks. Applies lightning to right-hand weapon.Affected weapon inflicts rare lightning damage, making it effective against targets which are resilient to both magic and fire. Very effective against dragon family foes
location: [1] - Treasure x3: Undead Burg, residence beside first crossbow sniper (tower). Requires Residence Key or Master Key. [2] - Sold by Domhnall of Zena (only 3) for 1,000 souls each. [3] - Sold by Elizabeth (only 5) for 1,000 souls each (AotA). [4] - Drops from Large Mushroom People (20% or 80% Chance - Darkroot Garden). [5] - Drops from Small Mushroom People (5% Chance - Darkroot Garden). [6] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the Red Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Royal Wood and Kiln of the First Flame).

description: Green weed, shaped like a flower. Temporary boost to stamina recovery speed.This uniquely bitter, biting herb is sometimes harvested in large quantities, but normally it is an annual plant found near water.
location: [1] - Sold by Crestfallen Merchant for 1,000 souls. (Unlimited amount.). [2] - Sold by Marvelous Chester for 1,200 souls (AotA) (Unlimited amount.). [3] - Drops from red Frog-Ray (15% or 25% Chance - Darkroot Garden).

description: Bone fragment reduced to white ash. Return to last bonfire used for resting.Bonfires are fueled by bones of the Undead. In rare cases, the strong urge of their previous owner's to seek bonfires enchants their bones with a homeward instinct.
location: [1] - Can be purchased from Undead Merchant (Female) for 500 souls. [2] - Can be purchased from Blacksmith Vamos for 500 souls. [3] - Can be purchased from Oswald of Carim for 500 souls. [4] - Can be purchased from Hawkeye Gough for 300 souls. [5] - Can be purchased from Marvelous Chester for 800 souls. [6] - Treasure x6 in chest at bottom of elevator shaft in Firelink Shrine. [7] - Video Location Timestamp 1:00. [8] - Video Location Timestamp 7:15. [9] - Video Location Timestamp 32:12.

description: Rare tiny black sprite found on corpses. Use to gain 1 humanity and restore a large amount of HP. This black sprite is called humanity, but little is known about its true nature.If the soul is the source of all life, then what distinguishes the humanity we hold within ourselves?
location: [1] - Sold by Undead Merchant (Female) for 5,000 souls (one time only). [2] - Sold by Patches the Hyena for 10,000 souls (only sells 3). [3] - Sold by Marvelous Chester for 10,000 souls (only sells 13) (AotA). [4] - Bribe from Patches, if you talk to him before the first incident, and don't forgive him after. [5] - Dropped by several NPCs. [6] - Dropped by most Bosses (patch 1.05). [7] - Dropped by Pisacas in Duke's Archive (4% Chance). [8] - Dropped by Humanity Sprites in Chasm of the Abyss (AotA) (Small: Single 4.44%, Twin 1.11%; Medium: Single 8%, Twin 2%; Large: Single 9.09%, Twin 4.55%).

description: Warm pebble emitting a beautiful phasing aura of seven colors, with a very rare eight.The rainbow stone does nothing special, but can serve as a path marker, and can be dropped off a cliff to judge height by the sound of descent.If a loud noise is heard upon its landing, then a fall off the ledge is surely lethal.
location: [1] - Sold by Undead Merchant (Female) for 10 souls. [2] - Sold by Patches the Hyena for 10 souls. [3] - Sold by Hawkeye Gough for 10 souls. [4] - Treasure x20: Chest in The Duke's Archives, beneath the staricase that leads to the Crystal Cave. [5] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the White Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Kiln of the First Flame and Royal Wood). [6] - Video Location Timestamp 32:22.

description: Ash-colored stone encasing a skull. Secret treasure of Arstor, the Earl of Carim. Reduces curse build-up and breaks curse.Humans are helpless against curses, and can only redirect their influence.The Purging Stone does not dispel curses, but receives them as a surrogate. The stone itself was once a person or some other being.
location: [1] - Sold by Undead Merchant (Female) for 6,000 souls. [2] - Sold by Oswald of Carim for 3,000 souls. He only has 5. [3] - Dropped by Man-Eater Shells (10% Chance) in Ash Lake or the Crystal Cave. [4] - 2 can be traded with Snuggly for a Cracked Red Eye Orb. [5] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the Red Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Royal Wood and Kiln of the First Flame).

description: Medicinal purple moss clump. Reduces poison build-up. Cures poison.Poison builds up in the body, and when it breaks out, it causes gradual damage over a period of time.Poison can be exasperating, so be certain to carry the needed quantity of these moss clumps when destined for an afflicted area.
location: [1] - Sold by Undead Merchant (Female) for 500 souls. [2] - Dropped by Ents in Darkroot Garden. [3] - Drops from Ents (20% chance - Darkroot Garden). [4] - Drops from Treant Gardener (Royal Wood). [5] - Drops from Blowdart Sniper (30% ~ 70% chance - Blighttown). [6] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the White Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Royal Wood and Kiln of the First Flame).

description: Lightly enchanted golden powder. Repairs weapon equipped in right hand.Use of a weapon depletes its durability, eventually rendering it ineffective.Repair Powder is a precious method of repair on the battlefield, as it obviates the need for a Repairbox.
location: [1] - Sold by Undead Merchant for 500. [2] - Sold by Elizabeth for 300 (AoTA Only). [3] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the White Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum,Royal Wood and Kiln of the First Flame).

description: Rotten pine resin that has gained poison. Adds poison effect to right-hand weapon.Hits with affected weapon build up enemy poison level. If enemy is poisoned, damage will be inflicted for a period of time
location: [1] - Sold by Undead Merchant (Female) for 1,000 souls.

description: Limb of the victim of a curse. Temporary curse allows engagement with ghosts.The only way to fight back against ghosts, who are cursed beings, is to become cursed oneself.The safest method, however dreadful, is to cut off an arm of the dead.
location: [1] - Sold by the Female Undead Merchant for 4,000 souls a piece. [2] - Sold by the Ingward for 1,000 souls a piece. (Patch 1.05). [3] - Dropped by ghosts in New Londo Ruins. [4] - Treasure x2: On corpse in a vase, before the rickety bridge to New Londo Ruins. [5] - Treasure x2: On corpse in a vase, behind a wall at the base of the first staircase in New Londo Ruins. [6] - Treasure x2: On corpse, behind screaming ghost close to the first fog-door. [7] - Drops from Banshee (New Londo Ruins, 5% chance). [8] - Drops from Ghost (New Londo Ruins, 5% chance). [9] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the White Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Royal Wood and Kiln of the First Flame). [10] - Video Location Timestamp 1:25.

description: Rare tiny black sprite found on corpses. Very unusual twin humanity. Use to gain 2 humanity and restore a large amount of HP.This black sprite is called humanity, but little is known about its true nature.If the soul is the source of all life, then what distinction is left for humanity?
location: [1] - Can be taken as a Gift upon character creation. [2] - Sold by: Patches the Hyena for 20,000 (1 only?). [3] - Atonement gift from Patches if you choose not to forgive him after the incident in the Tomb of Giants. [4] - Treasure: Lower Undead Burg, guarded by zombies with torches. [5] - Treasure: In a chest behind a fake wall, in tunnel between Blighttown and The Great Hollow. [6] - Treasure: Sniper tower above bonfire, in the lavabed by Lost Izalith. [7] - Dropped by Oswald of Carim when killed. [8] - Dropped by Giant Humanity spirits that consist of medium and small sizes (4.55% chance for Large, 2% for Medium and 1.11% for Small - Chasm of the Abyss). [9] - Dropped by some Bosses. [10] - Dropped by some Vagrants. [11] - Drops from Clan of Forest Protectors (Guaranteed, but only drops from the non-respawning Archer (Pharis) - Darkroot Garden). [12] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the Red Evil Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Kiln of the First Flame and Royal Wood). [13] - Video Location Timestamp 3:38. [14] - Video Location Timestamp 0:13. [15] - Video Location Timestamp 3:22. [16] - Video Location Timestamp 4:15.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. Chaos Flame Ember is an art of the lost city of Izalith. Handled only by blacksmiths knowledgeable in ancient methods. Ascends +5 flame weapon to chaos weapon. (chaos weapon can be reinforced to +5) Chaos weapons are demon weapons augmented by humanity which inflict fire damage.
location: [1] - Found after beating Ceaseless Discharge, which is located right after the boss in Blighttown, Chaos Witch Quelaag. [2] - After you kill him, the lava will harden allowing you to walk to the area he was standing. The Ember is located in a pool of lava (not the item against the rock-wall), and is guarded by 7-8 or so Taurus Demons, so you better take them on one at a time. [3] - Use of the Flame Stoneplate Ring and the Flash Sweat spell is recomended, because you'll actually have to walk through the lava to get to the Ember. Also, the Gold-Hemmed Robe set provides great fire resistance, and can be looted from a corpse near Ceaseless Discharge. [4] - The Orange Charred Ring, dropped by the Centipede Demon, can be used to safely acquire the Ember. [5] - Video Location Timestamp 1:00.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. The Crystal ember was created by Seath the Scaleless. Can only be handled by the giant God's blacksmiths. Ascends +10 standard weapon to Crystal weapon; Crystal weapons can be reinforced to +5. Crystal weapons are powerful but brittle, and cannot be repaired.
location: [1] - The Crystal Ember is found behind the bookshelves, in the same room that leads out and down to the crystal cave. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 31:49.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. The church long hid the forbidden black ember, and no living blacksmith knows of it.Ascends +5 divine weapon to occult weapon. (occult weapon can be reinforced to +5)Occult weapons were used to hunt the Gods, and are effective against their followers and kin.
location: [1] - Painted World of Ariamis. This Ember is found inside the Annex, for which you need the Annex Key to enter. [2] - It is guarded by two Engorged Zombies.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. Divine embers are property of the church, and intended for divine blacksmiths. Ascends +5 standard wpn to divine weapon.(divine weapon can be reinforced to +5) Divine weapons are for Undead hunting. Use against Undead and the pawns of necromancers.
location: [1] - Divine Ember can be found after the boss fight with the Moonlight Butterfly on top of a tower. The ember is held by a petrified Blacksmith. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 7:15.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. This enchanted ember, a form of sorcery, is a vestige of the lost land of Oolacile. Ascends +5 magic wpn to enchanted weapon.(enchanted weapon can be magic reinforced to +5) The sorcerer's enchanted weapon that inflicts magic damage and is boosted by intelligence.
location: [1] - Found in Darkroot Garden (second area; located pass Alvina) in a small pool guarded by Large Mushroom People. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 9:51.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. Large divine ember, of secret church rites. Intended for select divine blacksmiths. Ascends +5 divine weapon. Allows reinforcements to +10,on par with the very weapons of the Gods.
location: [1] - Obtained from a dead blacksmith who is among several giant skeletons in Tomb of Giants. [2] - From the first Bonfire (near Patches) climb up the ladder and go right, then follow the narrow path until you get to a big tomb. Enter by climbing down the ladder in a small opening. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 5:07.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. A large, high-quality ember. Handled by the blacksmith of Astora. Ascension for +5 standard weapon. Allows reinforcement to +10. Also, can ascend +5 std wpn to raw wpn. (raw weapon can be reinforced to +5)
location: [1] - Located in the Depths, continue down the path until you reach the kitchen. There you will find the chest which is located along the back wall behind the butcher's table. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 1:36.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. Large flame ember used in ancient rites. Handled only by blacksmiths knowledgeable in ancient methods.Ascends +5 fire weapon. Allows reinforcement to +10, the most powerful level for fire weapons
location: [1] - This ember is in a chest at the end of a hallway on the left, near the entrance to the Demon Firesage, and the shortcut to Lost Izalith. [2] - As you descend the stairs past the fire-breathing clay statues, you will see a Taurus Demon on a path to the left. Defeat it, and then defeat second one behind it to gain access to the hallway. Be wary, a centipede Demon pops out of the roof and will grapple you (typically a 1-shot). Defeat it and then dash to the chest. 4 more centipedes will spawn from the roof and create a deadly trap, your best bet is to run past, get the ember and use the homeward bone to escape. Otherwise bring a bow and lots of (heavy or poison) arrows. Also, for the arrow-tactic on the other hand, it is best to sneak through the hallway, as on that you will have left enough space between you and the worms every time. Even in the 4-worm death trap. Another option is to use a spear, as they have enough range to hit the worms without triggering their attacks. [3] - Bring a homeward bone, it makes the return process much easier.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. Large magic ember, a form of sorcery. Handled only by the Vinheim blacksmiths.Ascends +5 magic weapons. Allows reinforcement to +10, the most powerful level for magic weapons
location: [1] - The Duke's Archives. After defeating Seath the Scaleless, return to the archives where you first confronted Seath and open the chest in the middle of the room.

description: Ember required for weapon ascension. Huge ember of highest quality. Handled by the blacksmiths of Astora. Ascension for +10 standard weapon. Allows reinforcement to +15,on par with the greatest weapons of legend.
location: [1] - New Londo Ruins. After lowering the water level, go up the staircase found in a corner of the room with all the wooden cubicles. The ember is located in a chest on the third floor. [2] - Can be accessed relatively easily by taking the first elevator in the New Londo down, you shouldn't have to deal with too many enemies besides a few ghosts and the one Darkwraith that may chase you. [3] - The chest can also be reached by dropping down from the spot you encounter the first screaming female ghost (guarding some transient curse), after lowering the water level. This method allows you to bypass all the darkwraiths, since you'll land on a ledge on the second floor when dropping down. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 15:47.

description: Tool used to etch titanite into armor for reinforcement.Rest at a bonfire to make armor reinforcement possible
location: [1] - Sold by Andre of Astora for 2,000 souls. [2] - Sold by Blacksmith Vamos for 2,000(?) souls.

description: A peculiar bottomless wooden box. Its origins are unknown. Some deride it as a symbol of unbridled avarice.Any number of items can be deposited into the box, and items can be managed while resting at a bonfire
location: [1] - Sold by Domhnall of Zena for 1,000 souls. [2] - Sold by Undead Merchant (male) for 1,000 souls. (patch 1.05).

description: Lordvessel bestowed upon the chosen Undead who is destined to succeed Lord Gwyn.The chosen Undead is granted the art of warping between bonfires.To open the final door, place this vessel on the Firelink Altar, and fill it with powerful souls.
location: [1] - Firelink Shrine. [2] - Altar of Sunlight (Across the bridge over Undead Burg, guarded by the Hellkite Wyvern). [3] - Chamber of the Princess (Anor Londo). [4] - Anor Londo (Darkmoon Knightess room). [5] - Darkmoon Tomb (Anor Londo). [6] - Altar of the Gravelord (Gravelord Nito's Tomb, only accessible after defeating Nito). [7] - Daughter of Chaos (Queelag's Domain). [8] - Stone Dragon (Ash Lake). [9] - Painted World of Ariamis. Note: the player can only warp-to this location and cannot warp-from it. [10] - The Abyss (After defeating Four Kings). [11] - Depths (locked room by slime hallway, close to a Humanity farming spot). [12] - Undead Parish (By Blacksmith Andre & the entrance to Sen's Fortress). [13] - Tomb of the Giants (The first bonfire overlooking Demon Ruins in the distance, right next to Patches). [14] - The Duke's Archives (On the library balcony overlooking the Crystal Cave). [15] - Crystal Cave (After defeating Seath the Scaleless). [16] - Sanctuary Garden*. [17] - Oolacile Sanctuary*. [18] - Oolacile Township*. [19] - Chasm of the Abyss*.

description: Specialized mortar used to repair weapons and armor by grinding repair powder.Rest at a bonfire to make weapon and armor repair possible. Repair powder is fragile, and cannot be taken along
location: [1] - Sold by the Undead Merchant for 3,000 souls. [2] - Sold by Blacksmith Andre for 2,000 souls.

description: This secret rite allows bonfires to be bolstered further with Kindling, so that even more Estus can be collected.Kindling was a sacred rite passed down among clerics, but all Undead can imitate the process, in the same manner that they restore their Hollowing with humanity.How peculiar that humans had found little use for humanity until they turned Undead.
location: [1] - The "Rite of Kindling" is learned by defeating Pinwheel in The Catacombs. [2] - Reah of Thorolund and her companions seemed to be searching for the Rite of Kindling.

description: Soul of the Bed of Chaos and the mother of all demons. This Lord Soul was found at the dawn of the Age of Fire.The Witch of Izalith attempted to duplicate the First Flame from a soul, but instead created a distorted being of chaos and fire. Its power formed a bed of life which would become the source for all demons, and is more than enough to satiate the Lordvessel
location: [1] - Drops from The Bed of Chaos.

description: Soul of one of the Four Kings, who fell to Dark. A fragment of a Lord Souls discovered at the dawn of the Age of Fire Lord Gwyn recognized the foresight of these four great leaders of New Londo, and granted them their ranks and the fragments of a great soul. Although this is not a full Lord Soul, it can still satiate the Lord Vessel
location: [1] - Drops from The Four Kings.

description: Soul of Gravelord Nito, first of the dead. This Lord Soul was discovered at the dawn of the Age of Fire.Gravelord Nito administers the death of all manner of beings. The power of this soul is so great that it satiates the Lordvessel, despite the fact that much of its energy has already been offered to death.
location: [1] - Drops from Gravelord Nito.

description: Soul of the albino Seath the Scaleless. A fragment of a Lord Soul discovered at the dawn of the Age of Fire. Seath allied with Lord Gwyn and turned upon the dragons, and for this he was awarded Dukedom, embraced by the royalty, and given a fragment of a great soul. Although just a piece, it will still satiate the Lordvessel.
location: [1] - Drops from Seath the Scaleless.

description: Tool used to etch titanite into weapons for reinforcement.Rest at a bonfire to make weapon reinforcement possible
location: [1] - Sold by Andre of Astora for 2,000 souls. [2] - Sold by Blacksmith Vamos for 2,000(?) souls.

description: Key to the annex in the Painted World of Ariamis.In the wintery painted world, there is a structure resembling an old cathedral; its annex serves as a type of storehouse
location: [1] - Found in the underground sewers section in Painted World of Ariamis, located in a dead end behind an illusory wall. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 26:17.

description: Extra key to a cell in the Duke's Archive Tower.Perhaps the serpent men were careless, for there are several keys scattered about which fit Archive Tower cells.
location: [1] - The Duke's Archives Tower. Found on corpse after dropping down from cell 3 to 4. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 11:46.

description: Key to the cell of the Duke's Archive Tower.The Archive Tower, once a trove of precious tomes and letters, became a prison after the onset of Seath's madness. The serpent men who guard the prison know not the value of what they hide. In the basement of the tower are the writhing "mistakes" of the terrifying experiments which were conducted there
location: [1] - The Duke's Archives Tower. Dropped by sleeping serpent guard. [2] - Drops from Man Serpent (100% Chance - The Duke's Archives). [3] - Video Location Timestamp 7:32.

description: Key to the giant cell below the Duke's Archives Tower. The giant cell once imprisoned countless maidens, but is now empty, save for a few key persons. They struggle to uphold their sanity, as the horde of "mistakes" writhe at a fearfully close proximity
location: [1] - Found in chest at bottom of Archives, by the exit to the Crystal Cave.

description: Key to the giant door leading out from the uppermost floor of Archive Tower, which now serves as a prison.The key's design resembles not the key of a prison; indeed, the tower was once no prison at all, but a trove of precious tomes
location: [1] - Archive Tower. Found in chest behind gramophone. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 15:12.

description: Opens the narrow passage leading below at the far face of the great bridge in the Undead Burg.The lower Undead Burg is a treacherous place. Do not turn your back on the wily thieves, or the wild dogs who serve the Capra Demon
location: [1] - Found on steps to the Undead Parish, right behind the big gate. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 4:57.

description: Key to the inner door of the Undead Asylum main hall. Big key belonging to a chosen Undead pilgrim. But this Chosen Undead knows not what this pilgrimage has in store
location: [1] - It is dropped by the Asylum Demon if you killed him when you are supposed to, during the second encounter. [2] - However, if the Asylum Demon is killed during the first encounter then this key will be dropped by the Elite Knight/Oscar of Astora when you either speak to him or kill him. [3] - Drops from Oscar of Astora. (Guaranteed - Northern Undead Asylum and Northern Undead Asylum Revisit). [4] - Video Location Timestamp 3:36.

description: Key to Blighttown from the Depths of the Undead Burg. Swallowed by the Gaping Dragon.As its name suggests, Blighttown is a place of great pestilence. Even the polluted inhabitants of the Depths are aware of its dangers, and built this mighty door in hopes that they could remain safely separated.
location: [1] - Depths. Dropped by Gaping Dragon.

description: Half of a broken stone pendant. The vine appears to originate from Oolacile.A powerful magic can be sensed from this ancient stone. Yet men of this time can neither manipulate nor sense its power, which has a distinct air consisting of both reverence and nostalgia.
location: [1] - Dropped by the Crystal Golem at the entrance to the Duke's Archives, right after the first elevator. Only after you have rescued Dusk of Oolacile. [2] - The Crystal Golem will appear regardless of whether or not you have rescued Dusk of Oolacile. However, The pendant will not drop if Dusk of Oolacile has not been rescued and spoken to. [3] - You must kill the Hydra before you can get the Broken Pendant from the Crystal Golem. (Drop chance guaranteed - Darkroot Basin, outside of The Duke's Archives and Crystal Cave). [4] - Video Location Timestamp 2:48.

description: Key to the hanging cage in Sen's Fortress. If a hapless adventurer becomes fatigued during an imprudent attempt to overcome the fortress, the serpent men will not kill him, but lock him up in a lonely cage. Eventually, unless they have forgotten, they drag the victim off to who-knows-where.
location: [1] - Sen's Fortress. At the bottom of the tower with Crestfallen Merchant. Jump across the broken bridge to access the tower. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 21:51.

description: The grooves of the crest are enchanted, the door sealed with a powerful spell.
location: [1] - Found in a mimic chest in the lower section of the Oolacile Township. When you reach a building with about 6 bloated residents and two casters, soon before you can unlock the shortcut, look for a staircase and follow it upwards. One the second level up you'll have a chance to look left and see an alcove with a burning fire. You can roll to access it, kill the mimic and obtain the key. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 17:44.

description: This crest opens a door in the Darkroot Garden sealed by ancient magic. The door leads to the grave of Sir Artorias the Abysswalker. Many adventurers have left for the grave, but none have returned, for they make easy prey for local bandits. With such dangers, the crest can do more harm than good in the hands of the uninitiated
location: [1] - Sold by Blacksmith Andre for 20,000 souls. [2] - Dropped by Blacksmith Andre if killed.

description: Key to the dungeon of the Undead Asylum to the North.A mysterious knight, without saying a word, shoved a corpse down into the cell, and on its person was this key. Who was this knight? And what was his purpose? There may be no answers, but one must still forge ahead.
location: [1] - Northern Undead Asylum. On corpse dropped into cell.

description: Key opening the door from the lower Undead Burg to the Depths. Those banished from the Undead Burg eke out their existence in the Depths, a damp lair with no trace of sunlight. Nearly half of the Depths form a perilous flooded labyrinth.
location: [1] - Lower Undead Burg. Dropped by Capra Demon.

description: Key to the iron bars separating the ruins of New Londo and Drake Valley.The ruins of New Londo were blocked off, for the cursed ghosts posed danger to life and spirit, and the legends speak of a terrible Dark which was sealed away
location: [1] - Blighttown. In a chest at the end of the cave, when entering from Valley of Drakes. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 12:02.

description: Key to the floodgates of New Londs, which seal away the Four Kings who fell to Dark.The Sealers flooded New Londo to banish the Darkwraiths and the Four Kings. The agonizing decision was made with the realization that countless lives, and the robust culture of the city, would be lost. The victims now roam the ruins as ghosts
location: [1] - New Londo Ruins. Gift from Ingward after getting the Lordvessel. [2] - Ingward will also drop it if killed, without the Lordvessel. [3] - !!!!IMPORTANT NOTE FOR PC VERSION!!!!: On the PC PTD Edition, killing Ingward is currently bugged, and he may not always drop the Key to the Seal. It is currently unknown what causes this, but the key becomes completely unrecoverable (quitting/loading or visiting the chest in Fire Link Shrine do not work) effectively preventing you from completing the game. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 8:07.

description: This universal key opens any basic lock. Tool of the trade for thieves.But in the cursed land of the Undead, most doors are better left unopened.
location: [1] - Thief class starting equipment. [2] - Chosen as a gift upon character creation. [3] - Sold by Domhnall of Zena for 5,000 souls, after he moves to the Bridge in Firelink Shrine. (Patch 1.04) This purchase will carry over into NG+.

description: The purpose of this key is unknown. It appears to be a basic prison cell key
location: [1] - Found behind the first ladder, after the armored boar, when going through the cellar in Undead Parish. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 2:54.

description: A strange doll in strange dress. There once was an abomination who had no place in this world. She clutched this doll tightly, and eventually was drawn into a cold and lonely painted world
location: [1] - It is found in the cell you start the game in after you return to the Northern Undead Asylum. It is guarded by a Black Knight.

description: Key to a residence in the Undead Burg. Simple, sturdy design of the locks in the Burg keep out unwanted visitors.But this is a standard key in these parts, and will open several residences
location: [1] - Undead Burg. Bought from Undead Merchant.

description: Key to the sewer chamber in the Depths. In any community, a few bad apples are sure to exhibit insatiable greed. If they were turned Undead, and banished to the Depths, would they reconsider their ways? Use this key to see for yourself
location: [1] - Depths. On a corpse in the room with the giant rat. The key is accessible from both sides of the iron bars. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 6:17.

description: Key to the iron bars on the east side of the second floor of the North Undead Asylum. The Undead Asylum is a giant Undead prison, segmented by countless iron bars. Even if an Undead were to escape from a cell, passage to the outside world would not be gained easily
location: [1] - The Elite Knight in the Northern Undead Asylum will give it to you during his dying moments, along with five Estus Flasks. [2] - The Elite Knight/Oscar of Astora also drops this key if you choose to put him out of his misery instead of adhering to his final wishes. [3] - Drops from Oscar of Astora. (Guaranteed - Northern Undead Asylum and Northern Undead Asylum Revisit).

description: Key to the iron bars on the west side of the second floor of the Northern Undead Asylum. The Undead Asylum is a giant Undead prison, segmented by countless iron bars. But even if a hero found a key in Lordran to liberate this prison, would he have the means, or the heart, to ever come back?
location: [1] - On the roof of the building which houses Petrus of Thorolund in the Firelink Shrine. [2] - It is accessible after unlocking the elevator short-cut back to the Firelink Shrine from the Undead Parish. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 4:35.

description: Key to the basement of the watchtower in the Undead Burg.The basement of the watchtower forms a stone cell. There are rumors of a hero turned Hollow who was locked away by a dear friend. For his own good, of course.
location: [1] - Darkroot Garden. On top of the tower, after crossing the Moonlight Butterfly bridge. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 7:15.

description: This black crystal, long a symbol of farewell, is granted to banished Undead.The crytal sends phantoms back to their homes, or sends you back to yours.Beware of fickle use of this item if you intend to nurture relations
location: [1] - Present upon game start.

description: Online play item. Invade world of player in Book of the Guilty. Subdue player to acquire Souvenir of Reprisal. (Only Covenanter can use the item) These mystical orbs are granted to Blades of the Darkmoon, knights who serve the Dark Sun Gwyndolin, so that they may serve the Gods in meting out vengeance.
location: [1] - Given to the player upon joining Blade of the Dark Moon covenant.

description: The goddess of Sin, Velka, oversees this list of the guilty, who have disrespected the Gods or their covenants, and shall one day face the wrath of the Blades of the Darkmoon.
location: [1] - This item can be bought from Oswald of Carim.

description: Invade another world. (Hollows cannot use the item)Defeat the master of the world you have invaded to acquire humanity.The Cracked Red Eye Orb allows players to temporarily imitate this ability normally limited to the Darkwraiths of Kaathe.
location: [1] - Sold by Darkstalker Kaathe for 3,000 souls. (see page for details). [2] - Treasure x4: In a chest below the elevator shaft in Firelink Shrine. [3] - Treasure x6: On a corpse hanging over a well in Lower New Londo Ruins.

description: Online play item. Find a player who has acquired a Dragon Scale and invade that player's world to pillage it.An art of the transcendent apostles who pray to the ancient dragons. To be alive is to be vulnerable, and the fiery Gods are no exception. The apostles seek another plane of existence, which transcends life
location: [1] - Gift upon joining the Path of the Dragon covenant. [2] - Only Dragon Covenant members can use the item.

description: Dried finger with multiple knuckles.Shriveled but still warm. With this many knuckles, surely it cannot belong to anything human.
location: [1] - Painted World of Ariamis. Drop down onto the balcony, from first left turret when facing the castle from the bonfire. [2] - In Dark Souls Remastered, the dried finger is now sold by the Undead Merchant in the Undead Burg, and the item from its previous location is a Twin Humanities. [3] - Note: Only one of this item can be held, leaving the player unable to pick it up in the next game cycle.

description: Online play item. Lure phantoms from other worlds. (Only Covenanter can use this item while Hollows cannot)The dreadful Eyes of Death spread disaster across neighboring worlds. Phantoms lured to the host world may end up as victims, allowing the Eyes of Death to multiply, and leading to further proliferation of bane.
location: [1] - 3 are obtainable in The Catacombs, behind a Titanite Demon. [2] - 3 are in the Tomb of Giants. Behind the 1st bonfire, it's up a path to the left of the ladder that goes to the Large Divine Ember. [3] - Patches the Hyena sells three of them for 3,000 Souls each. [4] - Drops from Basilisk (6% Chance - Depths, The Great Hollow and Ash Lake). [5] - Video Location Timestamp 1:52. [6] - Video Location Timestamp 2:07.

description: Slip sold by bishop of Velka, Goddess of Sin. If you are killed by an invader, use this to report the crime of that trespasser.The indicted player will be added to a list of unfortunate souls who will one day face the wrath of the Blades of the Darkmoon
location: [1] - This item can be bought from Oswald of Carim. . He will appear at the base of the bell-tower, in Undead Parish, after the Gargoyles are defeated and the bell is rung.

description: Online play item. Write/view/rate message.Messages transmit to other worlds, where they are rated. Also, rate messages of others.In Lordran, the flow of time is distorted, and messages allow Undead to assist (or deceive) one another
location: [1] - Sold by the Undead Merchant for 100 Souls. [2] - Dropped by the Undead Merchant when killed.

description: Online play item.Quit battle of Stoicism.(Session ends if host leaves)Victory in the battle once led to ancient Anor Londo, but even in the absence of its overseer, capitulation is a disgrace. In the name of a warrior's honor, do not quickly resort to use of this crystal.
location: [1] - After defeating Artorias and exiting the arena, take the right path and go forward.

description: Online play item. Invade another world. Defeat the master of the world you have invaded to acquire humanity.The Darkwraiths of Kaathe use this orb to seek humanity and plunge further into dark. Perhaps they are more human than we?
location: [1] - You receive this item after ranking up once with the Darkwraith covenant. [2] - Level one can be reached by offering 10 humanity to Darkstalker Kaathe. [3] - You may only use the Red Eye Orb as long as you are part of the Darkwraith covenant.

description: Be summoned to another world as a dark spirit, and defeat the summoner to acquire humanity.Certain Darkwraiths resist their descent into dark and preserve along the honorable path. The Red Soapstone is for them.
location: [1] - This item is found in the Painted World of Ariamis, on top of the spiral stairs inside the round tower. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 9:08.

description: Online play item. Check servants of the Daughters of Chaos. Egg-bearer Eingyi keeps this roster of those who serve his master, a Daughter of Chaos. Eingyi praises those who offer significant humanity to alleviate his master's suffering. But what is praise from Eingyi really worth?
location: [1] - Sold by Eingyi.

description: Blood-drained, shrunken ear. Souvenir taken from subduing the guilty.The knights called the Blades of the Darkmoon punish the guilty-soaked offenders of the Gods and take this as proof of their conquest. The earless corpses of the guilty will be left behind as a warning to others, inspiring both fear and respect for the Gods. Such is the eternal mandate of the Dark Sun
location: [1] - While being a member of the Blade of the Dark Moon, invade and kill a player who have committed sins, either with the Blue Eye Orb or the Darkmoon Blade Covenant Ring. [2] - Killing Lautrec by invading his world, in Anor Londo, after he's killed Anastacia of Astora. [3] - Offer a Pendant to Snuggly the Crow and you will receive a Souvenir of Reprisal in exchange. [4] - Drops from Crow Demon (6% Chance - Painted World of Ariamis). [5] - Location Video Timestamp 28:32.

description: This faintly warm medal, engraved with the symbol of the Sun, is the ultimate honor, awarded to show those who summon the Warrior of Sunlight and complete a goal.The symbol represents Lord Gwyn's firstborn, who lost his deity status and was expunged from the annals. But the old God of War still watches closely over his warriors.
location: [1] - Location Video Timestamp 28:32.

description: Online play item. Leave summon sign.Be summoned to another world as a phantom through your sign, and defeat the area boss to acquire humanity.In Lordran, the flow of time is distorted, and the White Sign Soapstone allows Undead to assist one another
location: [1] - Gift from Solaire of Astora. [2] - Also available as a drop from Solaire if killed, but not recommended for he´ll become unavailable for further summoning.

location: [1] - Sold by Undead Merchant (Female) for 500 souls. [2] - Treasure above Armored Boar. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 1:56.

description: Black bisque urn filled with black powder. Explodes, inflicting fire damage.Powerful ranged weapon, especially in situations called for fire damage. A very precious item at low levels.Black Firebombs are especially destructive.
location: [1] - Receive 10 as an optional Gift upon character creation. [2] - Sold by Crestfallen Merchant for 500 souls. [3] - Sold by Marvelous Chester for 800 souls. [6] - These bombs (if conserved) can be very useful in Capra Demon's fight. They can be thrown at the demon from atop the staircase while he is slowly walking to the bottom of the stairs, dishing out a considerable chunk of his health before engaging in melee with him. Limited number of Black Firebombs can be compensated with normal Firebombs, making it possible to kill the Capra Demon without having to melee him at all.

description: Atrocious fecal waste material. Throw at enemy to build up toxins, but also builds up your toxicity.Although the stench makes it difficult to carry on one's person, turning an enemy toxic inflicts high damage over a period of time

[2] - The Duke's Archives: After you kill Seath The Scaleless, go back to the place you first fought him and go up some stairs and there will be a corpse.

description: Soul of a hero of legend who has long ago gone Hollow. Use to acquire a large amount of souls.Souls are the source of all life, and whether Undead, or even Hollow, one continues to seek them.
location: [1] - Sen's Fortress: Inside a hanging cage beside Big Hat Logan. [2] - Anor Londo: Guarded by the second of the infamous Silver Knight archers. [3] - Anor Londo: Balcony above the Titanite Demon, guarded by a Silver Knight. [4] - Crystal Cave: In front of the Moonlight Butterfly right after crossing the last invisible bridge. [5] - Tomb of Giants: There's two in the last area before the entrance to Nito. [6] - Lost Izalith: On a ledge above the underpass, close to the Rare Ring of Sacrifice. [7] - Video Location Timestamp 16:44. [8] - Video Location Timestamp 1:46. [9] - Video Location Timestamp 19:28. [10] - Video Location Timestamp 9:31. [11] - Video Location Timestamp 13:55. [12] - Video Location Timestamp 5:16.

description: Soul of a lost Undead who has long ago gone Hollow. Use to acquire souls. Souls are the source of all life, and whether Undead, or even Hollow, one continues to seek them
location: [1] - Drops from Corpse Wielder Infested Ghoul (20% chance - Blighttown). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 4:23. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 0:40. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 2:45. [5] - Video Location Timestamp 3:11. [6] - Video Location Timestamp 4:10. [7] - Video Location Timestamp 0:53. [8] - Video Location Timestamp 6:49.

description: Soul of a nameless Undead soldier who has long ago gone Hollow. Use to acquire many souls.Souls are the source of all life, and whether Undead, or even Hollow, one continues to seek them.
location: [1] - Video Location Timestamp 1:27. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 3:57. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 6:32. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 8:17. [5] - Video Location Timestamp 13:30. [6] - Video Location Timestamp 3:08.

description: Soul of a proud Undead knight who has long ago gone Hollow Use to acquire many souls.Souls are the source of all life, and whether Undead, or even Hollow, one continues to seek them.
location: [1] - Video Location Timestamp 0:40. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 4:36. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 4:46. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 6:13. [5] - Video Location Timestamp 14:20. [6] - Video Location Timestamp 4:29. [7] - Video Location Timestamp 1:16. [8] - Video Location Timestamp 5:00. [9] - Video Location Timestamp 12:20. [10] - Video Location Timestamp 13:18. [11] - Video Location Timestamp 13:25. [12] - Video Location Timestamp 17:58. [13] - Video Location Timestamp 13:26. [14] - Video Location Timestamp 2:17. [15] - Video Location Timestamp 2:52. [16] - Video Location Timestamp 22:54. [17] - Video Location Timestamp 23:16. [18] - Video Location Timestamp 23:18. [19] - Video Location Timestamp 28:29.

description: Soul of the mystical Moonlight Butterfly, which flitters in the Darkroot Garden. Special beings have special souls. The butterfly's soul is a creation of Seath the Scaleless. Use to acquire a huge amount of souls, or to create a unique weapon.
location: [1] - Obtained by killing the Moonlight Butterfly. (Guaranteed - Darkroot Garden). [2] - Note: the Crystal Butterflies in Crystal Cave do not drop boss souls. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 7:15.

description: Binoculars made of Brass. Use to peer at distant scenery.This advanced device was built by a famous craftsman of Astora. Its utility is singular, but its applications many.The value of these specs depends greatly on the imagination of their owner
location: [1] - In the Graveyard by Firelink Shrine. Keep right, along the mountain wall, located on a cliff edge just after 3 skeletons and 1 giant skeleton encounter. [2] - Only one of this item can be carried at a time, so place it in the Bottomless Box before picking up another. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 2:16.

description: Mystical orb found on a Keeper's corpse.Invade the world of the murderer of a Fire Keeper, to defeat the perpetrator and reclaim the soul of the Fire Keeper.The Black Eye keeps constant watch on the city of the Gods, Anor Londo
location: [1] - Found on corpse beneath the bonfire in Firelink Shrine.

description: The Darksign signifies an accursed Undead. Those branded with it are reborn after death, but will one day lose their mind and go hollow.Death triggers the Darksign, which returns its bearer to the last bonfire rested at, but at the cost of all humanity and souls.
location: [1] - Available from game start.

description: Stone imbued with the power of dragons. Rite of apostles of the ancient dragons. Gain head of dragon. Emit dragon breath.The dragon apostles seek ascendence of life itself, attainable by transformation into an ancient dragon.This rite is only one step, but it cannot be reversed until death
location: [1] - Gift from joining Path of the Dragon.

description: Stone imbued with the power of the dragons. Rite of apostles of the ancient dragons. Gain torso of dragon. Roar like a dragon.The dragon apostles seek transcendence of life itself, attainable by transformation into an ancient dragon. This rite is only one step, but it cannot be reversed until death
location: [1] - Offer 30 Dragon Scales to the Everlasting Dragon.

description: "One of the ancient treasures of Anor Londo. Presented to Artorias for facing the Abyss.Effectively deflects the Dark of the Abyss, especially in its magic forms."
location: [1] - Found in a chest, inside a hidden room in Oolacile Township. Use a light-source ( Cast Light spell, Skull Lantern and Sunlight Maggot confirmed) to reveal an illusory wall to access the room. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 3:18.

description: Skull Lantern of the Catacombs necromancer. Droops from his long beard locks. This lantern alights the Tomb of the Giants, Nito's light-devouring domain of death. Also serves as a fire damage strike weapon.
location: [1] - Rare drop (2%) from necromancers killed in the Catacombs. [2] - Found as a treasure in the Tomb of Giants, in the pit close to the first bonfire. This is the same pit that holds Patches' 'treasure.'. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 9:16.

description: Coin made of copper. Its face shows an image of Old Man McLoyf, god of medicine and drink.Even coins of great value in the world of men have little value in Lordran, where the accepted currency is souls.Those who dream of returning to the outside world are fond of carrying these around
location: [1] - Talk to Petrus of Thorolund a couple times before Reah arrives and he will give you one. [2] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the Red Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Kiln of the First Flame and Royal Wood)\.

description: Coin made of gold, with Allfather Lloyd and his white halo shown on its face.Even coins of great value in the world of men have little value in Lordran, where the accepted currency is souls.Those who dream of returning to the outside world are fond of carrying these around.
location: [1] - Drops from Mimic. (100% Chance - Sen's Fortress, Anor Londo, The Duke's Archives and Oolacile Township). [2] - Can be found in the Painted World of Ariamis hidden under some barrels near a ladder when coming up a building full of albino rats. [3] - Found on a corpse in Royal Wood, near three unaggressive Treant Gardeners (they will become hostile if you will loot the corpse). [4] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the Red Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Kiln of the First Flame and Royal Wood). [5] - Video Location Timestamp 17:51. [6] - Video Location Timestamp 12:16. [7] - Video Location Timestamp 19:45.

description: A simple pendant with no effect. Even so, pleasant memories are crucial to survival on arduous journeys."
location: [1] - Can be selected as a gift upon character creation. [2] - Can be found at the altar of the Undead Parish, late in the game, if you let Petrus kill Reah. [3] - Random reward for killing a host when invading through the Cat Covenant Ring in the Darkroot Garden.

description: Rubbish with no value.Who in their right mind would bother carrying this around? Perhaps you need help.
location: [1] - Treasure, found by dropping down to the lower area, right in front of the first fog door in Undead Burg. [2] - Possible reward from forest hunter covenant when a successful invasion has been completed. [3] - Possible starting item. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 5:08.

description: Coin made of silver, with the portrait of the legendary Knight King Rendal on its face.Even coins of great value in the world of men have little value in Lordran, where the accepted currency is souls.Those who dream of returning to the outside world are fond of carrying these around
location: [1] - Drops 5 pieces from Mimic. (100% Chance - Sen's Fortress, Anor Londo, The Duke's Archives and Oolacile Township). [2] - Drops from Vagrants, specifically the Red Good Vagrant. (Depths, Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Painted World of Ariamis, Darkroot Garden, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Tomb of Giants, The Great Hollow, Ash Lake, Demon Ruins, Lost Izalith, Sen's Fortress, Blighttown, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins Lower, New Londo Ruins Upper, The Duke's Archives, Crystal Cave, Northern Undead Asylum, Kiln of the First Flame and Royal Wood).

description: Special miracle granted to the maidens of Gwynevere, Princess of the Sun. Gradual HP restoration for self and vicinity.The miracles of Gwynevere, the princess cherished by all, grant their blessing to a great many warriors.
location: [1] - Drops from Pisaca (Guaranteed - The Duke's Archives). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 13:36.

description: Miracle granted to those bound by covenant to Gwyndolin, Lord Gwyn's lastborn. Boost right weapon with rays of Darkmoon. The power of the rays of the Darkmoon are manifested in vengeance, and the deeper the animus, the more devastating the attack.
location: [1] - Offer 10 souvenirs of reprisal to Gwyndolin to Rank up in the Darkmoon Covenant. As for covenant item, you can have it only once per character.

description: Outland miracle, foreign to the Way of White. Emit Shockwave.Considered an alternative branching of Force. Emits an expanding shockwave orb.
location: [1] - Gift from Siegmeyer of Catarina in Firelink Shrine, for helping him in Anor Londo.

description: Lost miracle, foreign to the Way of White. While active, no losses incurred upon death.The Way of White recognizes death itself, but admonishes the Undead. It is for this very reason that this miracle was lost..
location: [1] - This miracle cannot be acquired in-game, but data exists for Escape Death deep within the game's code.

description: Common miracle among cleric knights. Create shockwave.This quickly-acting miracle inflicts no damage, but propels foes back and defends against arrows. Cleric knights use this miracle when charging into enemy mobs.
location: [1] - Sold by Petrus of Thorolund for 4,000 Souls. [2] - Sold by Reah of Thorolund for 1,000 Souls.

description: Miracle known only by the servants of the first dead, Gravelord Nito. Giant Gravelord swords jut out in vicinity.Many have died, and many eyes were claimed to realize the Greatsword Dance, a Gravelord ritual known only by his closest servants.
location: [1] - Obtained by reaching Rank 1 in the Gravelord Servant covenant. You can obtain this spell only once per character.

description: Miracle known only by the servants of the first dead, Gravelord Nito. Giant Gravelord swords jut out in vicinity.Nito sleeps deep within the Giant Catacombs, quietly overseeing all death, and waiting for his servants to usher in the Eye of Death.
location: [1] - Recieved upon joining the Gravelord Servant Covenant. As for covenant item, you can have it only once per character. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 3:47.

description: Great miracle cast by advanced clerics. Restores high HP.Great Heal is a long tale, only learned by a select few. No caster will be disappointed by the bountiful life that it yields.
location: [1] - Sold by Reah of Thorolund.

description: Great miracle cast by advanced clerics. Restores high HP.Great Heal Excerpt borrows from only several verses of Great Heal. As a result, it can only be cast a stark few times.
location: [1] - Sold by Petrus or Thorolund for 10,000 Souls. [2] - Sold by Patches the Hyena for 10,000 Souls. [3] - Sold by Reah of Thorolund for 2,000 Souls.

description: Miracle passed down by those bound by the Warrior of Sunlight covenant. Hurl giant lightning spear.The weapon of the God of War, who inherited the sunlight of Lord Gwyn, but had respect only for arms, and nothing else.
location: [1] - Gift upon reaching rank 1 in the Warrior of Sunlight Covenant. As a covenant item, you can have it only once per character. [2] - Covenant Related. Offer 10 Sunlight Medals.

description: Miracle of Bishop Havel the Rock. Cover body in powerful def. magic coating.Havel the Rock, an old battlefield compatriot of Lord Gwyn, was the sworn enemy of Seath the Scaleless. He despised magic, and made certain to devise means of counteraction.
location: [1] - Ash Lake: in the tree with the Basilisks, accessed from a hollow branch on the outside. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 4:39.

description: Elementary miracle cast by clerics. Restores HP.To cast a miracle, the caster learns a tale of the Gods, and says a prayer to be blessed by its revelations.Heal is the shortest of such miraculous tales.
location: [1] - Sold by Petrus of Thorolund for 4,000 Souls. [2] - Sold by Reah of Thorolund for 1,000 Souls. [3] - Sold by Patches the Hyena for 5,000 Souls.

description: Great miracle cast by advanced clerics. Return to the last bonfire rested at.Would normally link to one's homeland, only the curse of the Undead has distorted its power, redirecting casters to a bonfire.Or perhaps for Undead, this serves as home?
location: [1] - Sold by Petrus of Thorolund for 8,000 Souls. [2] - Sold by Reah of Thorolund for 1,000 Souls.

description: Miracle of the black-haired witch Velka. Temporary auto counter vs. heavy damage.For each sin there is a punishment, and it is the task of Goddess Velka to define the sin, and mete out the punishment.
location: [1] - Sold by Oswald of Carim in the Undead Parish.

description: Miracle passed down to those bound by the Warrior of Sunlight covenant. Hurl lightning spear.Lightning spears inflict rare lightning damage, and are very effective against magic, fire, and most of all, dragons.
location: [1] - Gift upon joining the Warrior of Sunlight Covenant. As for covenant item, you can have it only once per character. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 10:01.

description: Miracle of Bishop Havel the Rock. Cover body in defensive magic coating.This coating greatly boosts magic defense, assisting warriors who must face the magic which Bishop Havel countered so proficiently.
location: [1] - Sold by Reah of Thorolund for 6,000 souls.

description: Common miracle amongst cleric knights. Gradual HP restoration.Beware of the cleric knight blessed by Replenishment, for he shall not fall easily.
location: [1] - Received from Reah of Thorolund after rescuing her from the two hollows in the Tomb of the Giants.

description: Miracle of clerics on an Undead mission. Display more guidance from other worlds.Guidance facilitates communication between Undead, but their value varies greatly. A balance of faith and wisdom is required.
location: [1] - Sold by Petrus of Thorolund for 2,000 souls. [2] - Sold by Reah of Thorolund for 500 souls.

description: Special miracle granted to the maidens of Gwynevere, Princess of the Sun. Restores high HP for self and vicinity.The miracles of Gwynevere, the princess cherished by all, grant their blessing to a great many warriors.
location: [1] - Drops from Pisaca (Guaranteed - The Duke's Archives). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 13:33.

description: Miracle wielded by Lord Gwyn's firstborn. Boost right weapon with rays of Sun.The power of sunlight, manifested as lightning, is very effective against dragons. When the eldest son was stripped of his deific status, he left this on his father's coffin, perhaps as a final farewell.
location: [1] - Darkmoon Tomb: go through the fog door to face Gwyndolin. Kill him and proceed to the next room to find this miracle in a chest. [2] - Be aware that going through the fog door breaks the Darkmoon covenant. Do not go through if you intend on being part of this covenant. This will also turn the Anor Londo Firekeeper hostile toward you.

description: Miracle born from the fading soul of Gwyn. Hurl sunlight spear.In the war that marked the dawn of the Age of Fire, Gwyn wielded these rays of sunlight, which remains fierce even as they fade.
location: [1] - Attain Warrior of Sunlight +1 and offer Soul of Gwyn, Lord of Cinder at Sunlight Altar.

description: Outland miracle, foreign to the Way of White. Slows all walking within effect area.This miracle is normally used to flee, as it slows walking but does not affect attacks, but nevertheless defines peace perfectly.
location: [1] - Catacombs - From the second bonfire, go across the bridge to the spiral tower with two skeletons. There's a breakable wall with a big skeleton behind it. Go through a hole on the right side of the room and follow it up until you get to a necromancer. There's an easily missable ladder to your right, past the doorway. The miracle is at the top of the ladder.Detailed Guide can be found here, as the miracle is on the way. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 22:46.

description: Secret rite of the black-haired witch Velka. Prevents casting of magic within effect area.Velka, the Goddess of Sin, is a rogue deity, but she is versed in arts both new and old, and is considered to have a great range of influence even as gods are concerned.
location: [1] - Painted World of Ariamis. On top of the square tower above the church, accessed by the Annex Key. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 16:50.

description: Primal form of Force. Create powerful shockwave.Wrath of the Gods was an epic tale that tattered over time and developed into the modern Force. This primal form of Force emits a shockwave that also inflicts damage.
location: [1] - Sold by Reah of Thorolund for 10,000 Souls.

description: Pyromancy foreign to the Great Swamp. Emit acid which corrodes weapons and armor.Not all pyromancy originates in the Great Swamp. One hears rumors of unknown pyromancers inhabiting various lands, and this spell is the work of one such outlander.
location: [1] - Near the edge of the cliff in the Painted World of Ariamis. In the graveyard area, to the right side of the stairs, as you enter. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 23:46.

description: A Pyromancy discovered from the Dark Abyss by a pyromancer who wandered into Oolacile. Conjure a great flame into one's palm.Black Flames are weighty and cause Physical damage. Enough to smack away the mightiest of shields.
location: [1] - Found on a corpse in the Chasm of the Abyss. (PTDE and Remastered only).

description: Art of the Flame of Chaos, which engulfed the Witch of Izalith and her daughters.Sweep foes with chaos fire whip which changes strength depending on humanity.The fire whip was wielded by the eldest of the Daughters of Chaos.
location: [1] - Chest in Lost Izalith, guarded by a Chaos Eater, by the entrance to Bed of Chaos. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 16:30.

description: Art of the Flame of Chaos, which engulfed the Witch of Izalith and her daughters.Erect localized chaos fire pillars which change strength depending on humanity.The Witch of Izalith, in an ambitious attempt to copy the First Flame, created instead the Flame of Chaos, a twisted bed of life.
location: [1] - Rank 2 in Chaos Covenant (30 humanities total).

description: Elementary pyromancy. Create powerful flame in hand.One of the most basic pyromancies. For this very reason, the flame thus created is fierce.
location: [1] - Laurentius at the Firelink Shrine after saving him in The Depths. [2] - Quelana (The Swamp in Blighttown).

description: Pyromancy which improves upon Fireball. Hurl fire orb.The flaming fire orb explodes upon impact, causing fire splash damage in a spectacle which seems to symbolize the strength of the fire-manipulating pyromancers.
location: [1] - Sold by Laurentius at the Firelink Shrine after saving him in The Depths for 8,000 souls. [2] - Sold by Quelana (The Swamp in Blighttown) for 8,000 souls.

description: Pyromancy foreign to the Great Swamp. Create a surge of flame from palm of hand.Not all pyromancy originates in the Great Swamp. One hears rumors of unknown pyromancers inhabiting various lands, and this spell is the work of one such outlander.
location: [1] - Located in Painted World of Ariamis. Dropped from the Bloated Dredge which can be found by either dropping through the well in the main courtyard or going through the invisible walls via the basement where the Annex Key is also found. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 25:27.

description: Primal pyromancy of Quelana of Izalith. Erect giant fire pillars in vicinity.The tempestuous raging flames resemble those summoned by the Daughters of Chaos when they challenged the ancient dragons and scorched the very earth.
location: [1] - Quelana gives it to you after finishing her favor. She will also drop it if killed.

description: Primal pyromancy taught by Quelana of Izalith. Sweep foes with fire whip.The root pyromancy of Combustion, but much more difficult to wield.
location: [1] - Quelana (The Swamp in Blighttown).

description: Standard pyromancy of the Great Swamp. Hurl fireball.The fire damage caused by fireballs makes them effective against corporeal beasts and Undead, who by nature fear flame.
location: [1] - The Pyromancer starts with this spell when the class is chosen at the start of the game. [2] - Laurentius at the Firelink Shrine after saving him in The Depths. [3] - Quelana (The Swamp in Blighttown).

description: Primal pyromancy taught by Quelana of Izalith. Erect fire pillars in vicinity.The storm of flame is indiscriminate, and incinerates all nearby life.
location: [1] - Quelana (The Swamp in Blighttown).

description: Pyromancy of Carmina, who harnessed the power of flame to actualize the inner-self. Intense sweating reduces flame damage.Carmina, the most accomplished pupil of Salaman the Master Pyromancer, pioneered new directions for the art of pyromancy.
location: [1] - Laurentius at the Firelink Shrine after saving him in The Depths.

description: Art of the Flame of Chaos, which engulfed the Witch of Izalith and her daughters.The Flame of Chaos can melt stone, producing a short-lived lava glob.
location: [1] - Joining Chaos Covenant (Corrupted Fire Keeper after Quelaag).

description: Pyromancy which improves upon Combustion. Create powerful, giant flame in hand.Great Combustion creates a powerful flame, but many sorcerers mock the simplicity of this spell.
location: [1] - Sold by Quelana (The Swamp in Blighttown) for 5,000 souls.

description: Ultimate fireball pyromancy. Hurl a giant fireball.Salaman the Master Pyromancer, also called the Great Fireball after this spell, believed pyromancy was rooted in an adoration of fire. Those who acquire this spell usually agree.
location: [1] - Quelana (The Swamp in Blighttown).

description: Pyromancy of Carmina, who harnessed the power of flame to actualize the inner-self. Iron Flesh boosts defense and resilience.Use of this pyromancy requires caution, as the caster becomes exceedingly heavy and unable to move freely.
location: [1] - Laurentius at the Firelink Shrine after saving him in The Depths.

description: Unique pyromancy crafted by Eingyi, considered a heretic even at the Great Swamp. Create poison mist.Eingyi became an egg-bearing Undead who serves a deformed young girl who speaks an untelligible tongue.
location: [1] - Swamp in Blighttown. [2] - Eingyi, the Egg-burdened npc near the Daughter of Chaos. You must speak to Eingyi while having an Egg for a head at least once in order for this pyromancy to be available for purchase. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 18:25.

description: Pyromancy of Carmina, who harnessed the power of flame to actualize the inner-self. Short strength/endurance boost, but lose HP.Excessive power eats away the life-force of its caster, and like all dangerous spells, Power Within was kept secret for eons.
location: [1] - Blighttown (Underneath the Parasitic Wall Hugger). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 8:39.

description: Unique pyromancy crafted by Eingyi, considered a heretic even at the Great Swamp. Create intense poison mist.Why was Eingyi driven from the Great Swamp? One only need cast this pyromancy, a perverse diversion from the art of fire, to find out.
location: [1] - Eingyi, the Egg Burdened NPC near the Daughter of Chaos. You must speak to Eingyi while having an Egg for a head in order for this pyromancy to be available for purchase.

description: Advanced pyromancy of Quelana of Izalith. Charm undead and gain temporary allies.The living are lured by flame, and this relationship is part and parcel to the art of pyromancy. Can be used by either gender.
location: [1] - Quelana (The Swamp in Blighttown).

description: Sorcery used by playful sorcerers. Lure enemies away by creating a sound originating in the distance.This spell is not the first choice for serious sorcerers, but it has a surprising amount of applications, some of them extremely effective.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim.

description: Ancient sorcery of the lost land of Oolacile. Cast a bright light upon surroundings. This light-producing sorcery is elementary but nonetheless demonstrates the achievements in mysticism of Oolacile. Such magic has not been developed even in Vinheim
location: [1] - Dusk of Oolacile.

description: Ancient sorcery of the lost land of Oolacile. Transform into something inconspicuous.A separate stealth spell from Hidden Body. A skilled stealth sorcerer must be aware of his or her surrounding and of which objects are prime candidates for imitation.
location: [1] - Dusk of Oolacile.

description: Sorcery boosted by the knowledge Logan acquired at the Regal Archives. High magic + crystallization: right weapon. Logan's trials were successful, and the crystal medium facilitated a stronger bond between weapon and soul.
location: [1] - Purchase from Big Hat Logan in Duke's Archives library (after freeing him from his cell).

description: Sorcery boosted by the knowledge Logan acquired at the Regal Archives. Fire piercing crystal soul spear.These pale magic spears, sharpened through crystallization, are on par with the armaments of the ancient lords.
location: [1] - Big Hat Logan.

description: Abyss sorcery discovered by an Oolacile sorcerer on the brink of madness. Fires successive Dark Orbs. In contrast to standard soul sorceries, Abyss sorcery is weighty and inflict physical damage. Perhaps a human soul is closer to matter in its humanity.
location: [1] - Found on a corpse behind an Oolacile Sorcerer in the Chasm of the Abyss. (AotA only). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 0:40.

description: Abyss sorcery discovered by an Oolacile sorcerer on the brink of madness. Create a cloud of Dark fog. Although Dark Fog is, in theory relatively close to humanity it also happens to be a terrible poison for humans. Perhaps to reflect man's cruelty against his own.
location: [1] - Found on a corpse in Oolacile Township. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 14:04.

description: Abyss sorcery discovered by an Oolacile sorcerer on the brink of madness. Fires Giant Dark Orb.In contrast to standard soul sorceries, Abyss sorcery is weighty and inflict physical damage. Perhaps a human soul is closer to matter in its humanity.
location: [1] - Found in Oolacile Township. (AotA only). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 6:52.

description: Sorcery developed by a certain surreptitious sorcerer at Vinheim Dragon School. Reduce damage and noise from fall.This sorcery, along with Hush, explains the extravagant cost of hiring Vinheim spooks.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim.

description: Sorcery which improves upon Heavy Soul Arrow. Fire strong heavy soul arrow.As difficult to use as Heavy Soul Arrow, but powerful enough to make it well worth it. Adored by certain sorcerers.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim. [2] - Big Hat Logan.

description: Sorcery which improves upon Magic Weapon. High magic augmentation: right weapon.At Vinheim Dragon School, only magic swordsmen on special orders are allowed to learn this spell which grants powerful augmentation to various weapons.
location: [1] - Cut the Chandelier on the catwalk and pick up in front of Painted World Entrance in Anor Londo. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 11:34.

description: Sorcery which improves upon Soul Arrow. Fire strong soul arrow.At the Vinheim Dragon School, the acquisition of this spell marks an apprentice's coronation as a sorcerer.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim. [2] - Big Hat Logan.

description: Soul sorcery emphasizing power. Fire heavy soul arrow.A more powerful, but also slower sorcery. Difficult to use due to a long cool down and a limitation on the number of castings.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim. [2] - Big Hat Logan. [3] - Blacksmith Rickert of Vinheim.

description: Ancient sorcery of the lost land of Oolacile. Turns body nearly invisible.Although perfect invisibility is unachievable due to the risk of dissipation, the caster need only stand still for a moment to blend in to environs with astounding camouflage.
location: [1] - Dusk of Oolacile.

description: Ancient sorcery of the lost land of Oolacile. Turns right weapon invisible.Not a simple augmentation, making it dependent on the skill of its caster. An example of the capacity of Oolacile sorceries to control light.
location: [1] - Dusk of Oolacile.

description: Sorcery boosted by the knowledge Logan acquired at the Regal Archives. Fire homing crystal soulmass.The mysteries of souls, crystals, and the sorceries are deeply intertwined.
location: [1] - Purchase from Big Hat Logan in Duke's Archives library (after freeing him from his cell).

description: Sorcery developed by Big Hat Logan: Homing soulmass. Life originates in the soul; no wonder the soulmass draws toward it. This sorcery is a window into seeker Logan's methods.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim. [2] - Big Hat Logan.

description: Sorcery developed by a certain surreptitious sorceror at Vinheim Dragon School. Masks all noises of caster.Effectively, Vinheim is controlled by the Dragon School, and it is no wonder that the town has its share of dark secrets.
location: [1] - Treasure: Sen's Fortress in the cell behind the Serpent guard, accessed from the middle of the snaking boulder path. [2] - Dropped by: Griggs of Vinheim when killed.

description: Sorcery for casters who wield swords. Magic augmentation: left shield.Shields augmented by magic are resistant to magic attacks and have higher stability. This spell makes it possible to challenge powerful foes with a small shield.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim. [2] - Big Hat Logan.

description: Sorcery for casters who wield swords. Magic augmentation: right weapon.The power of the magic swordsmen of Vinheim is predicated upon this and Magic Shield. Many warriors learn sorcery just for this.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim. [2] - Big Hat Logan.

description: Sorcery of Manus, Father of the Abyss. Grant a fleeting will to the Dark of humanity, and volley the result.The will feels envy, or perhaps love, and despite the inevitable trite and tragic ending, the will sees no alternative, and is driven madly towards its target.
location: [1] - Trade the Soul of Manus with Snuggly. (AotA Only).

description: Sorcery of the red-robed Yulva, one of the Sealers of New Londo. Reduce bleeding and poison build-up, and undo various poisonous effects.One of the New Londo's unique healing sorceries. Perhaps she abandoned her Sealer duty to take her healing arts back to Blighttown.
location: [1] - Blighttown, in a chest next to the Crimson Set (accessed by crossing the branch at the top of the waterwheel).

description: Ancient sorcery of the lost land of Oolacile. Repair equipped weapons and armor.This sorcery was part of everyday life in Oolacile. Its effects resembles repair powder.
location: NA

description: Sorcery of the red-robed remedician Ingward, guardian of the seal in New Londo. Sacrifice humanity to undo curse.Abhorrent curses eat away at the core of one's very existence, and cleansing oneself of curses is no easy task indeed
location: [1] - Ingward.

description: Elementary sorcery. Fire a soul arrow.Soul arrows inflict magic damage, making them effective against iron armor, tough scales, and other physically resilient materials
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim. [2] - Big Hat Logan. [3] - Blacksmith Rickert of Vinheim.

description: Sorcery developed by Big Hat Logan. Fire piercing soul spear.A symbol of Logan's strength, the soul spear is referenced repeatedly in the legends, and is said to be on par with Lord Gwyn's lightning.
location: [1] - Griggs of Vinheim. [2] - Big Hat Logan.

description: Sorcery which improves upon Magic Shield. High magic augmentation: left shield.At Vinheim Dragon School, only magic swordsmen on special orders are allowed to learn this spell which grants temporary strength to greatshields.
location: [1] - Chest in The Duke's Archives. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 22:36.

description: Sorcery developed by Logan during his infatuation with Seath the Scaleless. Emit crystal breath of Seath the Scaleless.Although it no longer causes curses, what madness caused old Big Hat to appropriate this frightful power of the ancient dragons?
location: [1] - Dropped by Big Hat Logan after he goes insane at the end of his complete quest line. [2] - Alternatively, the spell can be obtained before completing the quest line by killing passive Big Hat Logan after buying all of his spells. This will prevent the player from obtaining Logan's Catalyst and the remainder of the Big Hat's Set as killing him at this point fails the questline.

description: A special ring granted to only the most accomplished sorcerers at Vinheim Dragon School.The ring is engraved with an everlasting dragon, and boosts the strength of sorceries.
location: [1] - Can be bought from Griggs of Vinheim for 20,000 souls.

description: One of the infamous bite rings commissioned by Sir Arstor of Carim. Despite the dreadful rumorssurrounding its creation, this ring is an unmistakable asset, in its ability to help prevent bleeding.
location: [1] - Sold by Oswald of Carim in the Undead Parish for 10,000 souls.

description: The rare gem called tearstone has the uncanny ability to sense imminent death.This blue tearstone from Catarina boosts the defence of its wearer when in danger.
location: [1] - Undead Burg, guarded by a Black Knight. [2] - Can be looted by jumping from the bridge near the bonfire to the balcony where the ring is. [3] - Can also be looted from jumping down from where the hollows that throw firebombs are. If positioned right this is an easier jump to make than the bridge jump. Jump from approximately where the farthest left hollow stands. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 8:30.

description: A ring enchanted by the orange eye of Kalameet, the bringer of calamity. Doubles damage received by its wearer. A useless ring befitting of no finger. Best left unknown, or at least well hidden.
