location: [1] - Originally added with the Artorias of the Abyss DLC / bundle. [2] - Obtained after killing Black Dragon Kalameet.

description: Ring granted to those bound by the Forest Hunter covenant. Answer Alvina's summoning.The white cat Alvina speaks a human tongue and has lived since the early Age of Fire.Alvina was a trusted friend of Knight Artorias and the Great Wolf Sif.
location: [1] - Given to you by Alvina of the Darkroot Wood for joining the Forest Hunter covenant.

description: This ancient ring, engraved with a large green flower, is of unknown origin.Hastens the regeneration of stamina.
location: [1] - Found in a secret area near the middle of The Great Hollow by jumping into the middle of the tree. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 9:42.

description: This ring symbolizes Knight Artorias's covenant with the beasts of the Abyss.Its wearer, like Artorias himself can traverse the Abyss.
location: [1] - Awarded after killing the Great Grey Wolf Sif.

description: The serpent is an imperfect dragon and symbol of the Undead. Its habit of devouring preyeven larger than itself has led to an association of gluttony. This gold ring, engraved withthe serpent, boosts its wearer's item discovery, so that more items can be amassed.
location: [1] - Sen's Fortress. Get to the area with the boulder directing device and move the lever so that it goes west (same way as proper way to go to continue upstairs). Let the boulder roll 5-6 times so it fills up the hole then move the lever North (empty space). Now go into the West path and follow it through to the end. You will find a ring on a corpse in a window and a way into a room where the first mimic chest is. Video Location Timestamp 9:22.

description: The serpent is an imperfect dragon and symbol of the Undead. Its habit of devouring preyeven larger than itself has led to an association of gluttony. This silver ring, engraved withthe serpent, rewards its wearer with additional souls for each kill.
location: [1] - Found in Tomb of the Giants, close to the second Bonfire. [2] - After encountering and killing the 2nd skeleton dog there's a skeleton archer trying to shoot at you. After killing the archer, drop down by the corpse behind him, and there's a hidden path to the ring (drop a Prism Stone off the edge to see the path). [3] - It's also possible to jump up to the ledge, where the ring is located, on the right side. [4] - Note: Be wary that after picking up the ring 5 skeleton towers will attack you.

description: One of the infamous bite rings commissioned by Sir Arstor of Carim. Despite the dreadful rumorssurrounding its creation, this ring is an unmistakable asset, in its ability to help prevent curses.
location: [1] - New Londo Ruins, on a ledge at the backside of the house where Ingward dwells. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 7:04.

description: This special ring crafted in an Eastern land is made of gold, but with a wood grain creston its surface. Agents of subterfuge in this faraway land are particularly fond of thedark gold wood grain, which greatly alters its wearer's rolling action.
location: [1] - Drops from the ninja bodyguard that follows the merchant Shiva of the East in Darkroot Garden and Blighttown.

description: Ring granted to those bound by the Darkmoon Blade covenant. Answer Dark Sun Gwyndolin's summoning.Gwyndolin, all too aware of his repulsive, frail appearance, created the illusion of a sister Gwynevere, whohelps him guard over Anor Londo. An unmasking of these deities would be tantamount to blasphemy.
location: [1] - Granted upon joining the Darkmoon Blade Covenant.

description: This ring is granted to adherents of Gwyndolin, Darkmoon deity and last born of Gwyn, Lord of Sunlight.Grants additional magic attunement slots. The Dark Sun Gwyndolin is the only remaining deity in AnorLondo. His followers are few, but their tasks are of vital importance.
location: [1] - Note: The exit is about halfway back down the hallway, on the right coming in, on the left leaving. There are a few skeletons on the way, and a skeleton at the end of the passage. In the room where you can drop down (contains the Necromancer), there is a ladder to the right (when entering). Up the ladder is Miracle: Tranquil Walk of Peace. [2] - Video of location here. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 10:16.

description: This magic crown-shaped ring was granted to princess Dusk of Oolacile upon her birth.The ringstone allows its wearer to cast additional sorceries, but at the cost of one-half of HP.
location: [1] - Acquired by defeating the Hydra in Darkroot Basin.

description: This special ring crafted in an Eastern land is made of gold, but with a wood grain crest on its surface. The ring slows the loss of weapon durability and is particularly useful to bearers of delicate swords crafted in the East.
location: [1] - Purchase from Shiva of the East for 10,000 souls.

description: Stoneplates, the symbol of a true knight, grant the strength to face various hardships.The red stoneplate symbolizes fire, and boosts defence against flame.
location: [1] - Sen's Fortress, after passing through the castle, you go outside and have to go across pillars, before those pillars are stairs going down. Two Balder Knights will be guarding a chest. The ring is there. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 19:32.

description: This ring was named after Havel the Rock, Lord Gwyn's old battlefield compatriot.Havel's men wore the ring to express faith in their leader and to carry a heavier load.
location: [1] - Obtained after defeating Havel The Rock in the shortcut between Undead Burg and Darkroot Basin, that can be accessed with either Master key or Watchtower Basement Key.

description: One of the special rings granted to the four knights of Gwyn. The Hawk Ring belonged to Hawkeye Gough, who led the Greatarchers. Boosts bow range, so that arrows fly like they were shot by Gough's great bow, which took down high-flying dragons.
location: [1] - Found in Anor Londo, in a chest behind the Giant Blacksmith. It is not necessary to kill the Giant Blacksmith, simply roll through the gap to his left.

description: One of the special rings granted to the four knights of Gwyn. The Hornet RingBelonged to the Lord's Blade Ciaran. By boosting critical attacks, its wearer canannihilate foes, as Ciaran's dagger laid waste to Lord Gwyn's enemies.
location: [1] - Found in the area where the Great Grey Wolf Sif fight takes place, located behind the tombstone on which Sif makes his appearance.

description: One of the special rings granted to the four knights of Gwyn. The Leo Ring belongedto Ornstein the Dragonslayer. This ring strengthens counters with pierce weapons.His lugged spear is said to have sliced a boulder in two.
location: [1] - During the Ornstein and Smough boss fight, kill Ornstein last and he should drop the ring. You don't get it automatically, you have to pick it up.

description: A special ring granted to only the most accomplished sorcerers at Vinheim Dragon School.The ring is engraved with a lingering dragon, and boosts the length of the effects of sorceries.
location: [1] - Can be bought from Griggs of Vinheim for 20,000 souls.

description: Old ring from an old witch. Engraved minutely with indecipherable script, but seemingly useless.
location: [1] - Trading a Sunlight Maggot to Snuggly. [2] - Can also be selected as a starting gift.

description: An orange ring enchanted by a witch. Reduces lava damage. Since his sores were inflamed by lava from birth, his witch sisters gave him this special ring. But fool that he is, he readily dropped it, and from that spot, a terrible centipede demon was born.
location: [1] - Dropped by the Centipede Demon.

description: One of the infamous bite rings commissioned by Sir Arstor of Carim. Despite the dreadful rumorssurrounding its creation, this ring is an unmistakable asset, in its ability to help prevent poison.
location: [1] - Sold by Oswald of Carim in the Undead Parish for 15,000 souls.

description: This mystical ring was created in a sacrificial rite of Velka, the Goddess of Sin.The magenta-shaded ring is especially rare. Its wearer will lose nothing upon death,and will be freed from any curse whatsoever, but the ring itself breaks.
location: [1] - Sen's Fortress, in the tower with Ricard the Archer. [2] - New Londo Ruins, on the roof near Ingward. [3] - Lost Izalith, on a branch right below a ledge. [4] - Trade one Twin Humanities with Snuggly to receive one Rare Ring of Sacrifice. [5] - Video Location Timestamp 24:04.

description: The rare gem called tearstone has the uncanny ability to sense imminent death.This red tearstone from Carim boosts the attack of its wearer when in danger.
location: [1] - Valley of Drakes, above the giant gate. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 3:39.

description: A ring symbolizing the favor and protection of the goddess Fina, known in legend to possess "fateful beauty." This ring boosts its wearer's HP, stamina, and max equipment load, but breaks if ever removed.
location: [1] - Dropped by Lautrec after you kill him. [2] - Also received from trading the Xanthous Crown to Snuggly. [3] - Location Video Timestamp 28:32.

description: Those who befriend Alvina are given this mysterious ring. It resembles a pearl with its robust pure-white fog.The ring camouflages its wearer's presence, helping to prevent detection.
location: [1] - Given as a Forest Hunter Covenant reward for killing 3 invaders. [2] - Trade a Skull Lantern with Snuggly the Crow.

description: This mystical ring was created in a sacrificial rite of Velka, the Goddess of Sin. Its wearer will lose nothing upon death, but the ring itself breaks.
location: [1] - Found on the way from Firelink Shrine to Undead Burg on a corpse in plain view on the lower level of the aqueduct.. [2] - Found on the ledge above the well, in the central courtyard of Painted World of Ariamis. [3] - Sold by Oswald of Carim in the belltower of Undead Parish for 5,000 souls. He has a limited supply of 10. [4] - Trade one Humanity with Snuggly to receive one Ring of Sacrifice. [5] - Video Location Timestamp 3:20. [6] - Video Location Timestamp 20:00.

description: This ring belonged to the Knight King Rendal. It grants its wearer protection by boostingdefense against physical attacks. Of the many legends surrounding the Knight King Rendal,one of the more well-known speaks of his standing down a giant drake and slashing it to pieces.
location: [1] - Sen's Fortress at the top of the first rolling boulder area. A Serpent Mage is standing in the entrance, and the ring is in a chest behind him. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 6:52.

description: According to legend, this ring contains the spirit of the evil eye, a dark beast which assaulted Astora.The strength of the evil eye does not waver, and HP is absorbed from fallen enemies.
location: [1] - Located in the Depths, in the area with the basilisks. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 15:27.

description: This ring is granted to those who enter a Covenant with Gwynevere, daughter of Lord Gwyn and the Princess of Sunlight. This slightly warm ring boosts the synergy of miracles. The Princess of Sunlight left Anor Londo along with many other deities, and later became wife to Flame God Flann.
location: [1] - Gift from Gwynevere upon joining the Princess Guard Covenant. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 27:47.

description: Lord Gwyn's firstborn, who inherited the sunlight, once wore this ancient ring.Lord Gwyn's firstborn was a god of war, but his foolishness led to a loss of the annals, and rescinding of his deific status.Today, even his name is not known.
location: [1] - Found in Anor Londo, at the bottom of the rotating bridge by the bonfire (Darkmoon Tomb). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 10:12.

description: This iron ring was used to shackle the guilty. It is terribly rusted, and faintly stained with blood.Those who find this strange ring to their liking will be pleased to find it easier to gain footing on poor ground such as swamps.
location: [1] - It is at the edge of the decrepit stairway behind the locked door on the third level's west wing, guarded by two Undead Soldiers, in the Northern Undead Asylum.

description: This ring was secretly worn by a surreptitious sorcerer at Vinheim Dragon School.The ring is engraved with an everlasting dragon in silent slumber, and masks all sound emitting from its wearer.
location: [1] - Found in Sen's Fortress, on a rafter overlooking the first bridge with swinging blades. This area is accessed by dropping onto a walkway inhabited by a Snake-Headed Mage above a portion of the second ramp with rolling boulders that the player encounters. After dropping onto this walkway and dealing with the Serpent Mage, go through a broken hole in the wall at one end of the platform, pass through a small jail cell, and drop down onto the rafters below the far wall of the jail cell. Turn left and follow the rafter to a small corner, where the ring is located on a corpse. [2] - Also dropped by Griggs of Vinheim.

description: Stoneplates, the symbol of a true knight, grant the strength to face various hardships. The precious rare speckled stoneplate grants a small boost to defense against magic, flame, and lightning.
location: [1] - Dropped or given by Siegmeyer of Catarina.

description: Stoneplates, the symbol of a true knight, grant the strength to face various hardships.The blue stoneplate symbolizes souls, and boosts defense against magic.
location: [1] - Sold by Crestfallen Merchant in Sen's Fortress for 15,000 souls.

description: Stoneplates, the symbol of a true knight, grant the strength to face various hardships.The yellow stoneplate symbolizes thunder, and boosts defence against lightning.
location: [1] - Sold by Crestfallen Merchant in Sen's Fortress for 15,000 souls.

description: Ring made of an ancient tiny red jewel. Grants small increase to HP. Rings grant powers large and small. Their discovery, and effective use, can make one's journey easier
location: [1] - Can be selected as a starting gift. [2] - Reward from Siegmeyer of Catarina after defeating nearby Silver Knights in Anor Londo.

description: A divine ring entrusted to the head bishop of the Way of White and apostle to Allfather Lloyd, uncle to Lord Gwyn.It grants additional attunement slots.The head bishop of the Way of White is the guardian of law and caste, and one of the great royals of Thorolund.
location: [1] - Found in the The Duke's Archives. After being imprisoned, climb the ladder leading to the prison's exit. Before going through however, look left and you should see thin wooden planks with rods sticking out the sides. Drop down onto the second line of wooden planks and it will lead to a room containing a corpse. Looting this corpse will give you this ring and the Maiden Set.

description: One of the special rings granted to the four knights of Gwyn. The Wolf Ring belongs to Artorias the Abysswalker. Artorias had an unbendable will of steel, and was unmatched with a greatsword.
location: [1] - Notes and tips go here.

description: "Greatshield of the mighty knight Tarkus" "Built of special black iron and even heavier than Knight Berenike's tower shield. Especially resistant to fire attacks and effective for shield bashing."
location: [1] - Found on Iron Knight Tarkus' corpse in the grand painting hall of Anor Londo along with his matching armor set. He's on the ground floor, huddled in a corner to the left of the painting.

description: "Giant wooden wheel encircled by spikes. Half-melded to a strange skeleton" "Can be used as a great shield, only for the great gaps. But at least the spikes turn into weapons with a good spin. Strange indeed!"
location: [1] - Drops from Wheel Skeletons (2% chance - The Catacombs and Painted World of Ariamis). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 25:12.

description: The steel greatshield used by Knight Artorias, who succumbed to the Abyss. Artorias, deeply scarred by the Abyss, used this to form a barrier to protect his compatriot Sif. Although this drained the shield, its magic defense remains high.
location: [1] - Rescue Sif from the Chasm of the Abyss. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 2:00.

description: "Greatshield with an eagle design. Lightest and easiest greatshield to handle." "Greatshields are very stable, and deflect attacks. But one cannot parry, and instead bashes with the shield."
location: [1] - Blighttown treasure, found on a rafter close to the upper area Bonfire. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 6:35.

description: Greatshield made of bronze and marcasite. Used by the giant sentinels of Anor Londo. Marcasite, or white iron, is effective against non-physical damage, but as the shield is designed for the giant sentineles, its weight is unbearable."
location: [1] - Buy from the Giant Blacksmith for 10,000 souls. [2] - Drops from Sentinel (1% chance - Anor Londo). [3] - Drops from Royal Sentinel (1% chance - Anor Londo).

description: Shield born from the soul of the great grey wolf Sif, guardian of the grave of the Abysswalker Knight Artorias. Boasts consistent defense and divine protection against various status effects.
location: [1] - Ascend a +10 shield with the Soul of Sif.

description: "Greatshield of the legendary Havel the Rock. Cut straight from a great slab of stone." "This greatshield is imbued with the magic of Havel, proves a strong defense, and is incredibly heavy. A true divine heirloom on par with the Dragon tooth."
location: [1] - One of the chests behind the illusionary wall in Anor Londo.

description: Mossy round greatshield used by the guardian of the Darkroot garden, the Stone Knight." "The Stone Knight is a creation of ancient magic, and this shield is imbued with the same power, but is also extremely heavy."
location: [1] - Drops from the Great Stone Knight (2% chance - Darkroot Garden).

description: "Metal Greatshield. Used by the Knight Berenike, known for his heavy armour. This plate of iron is heavily defensive but ultra heavy. Greatshields are very stable, and deflect attacks. However, one cannot parry, and instead bashes with the shield."
location: [1] - Drop: Tower Knights in Undead Parish, Sen's Fortress and the Painted World (respawns). [2] - Purchase: Crestfallen Merchant for 8000 souls.

description: "Very small round metal shield. Parry attacks with large center protrusion. Small shields are always less stable, but landing critical hits after parry is easier. This shield is specialized for parrying."
location: [1] - Purchase from the Undead Merchant for 800 souls. [2] - Drops from Balder Knight with Rapier (1% chance - Sen's Fortress and Undead Parish).

description: Round wooden shield crafted in Lordran. Decorated by an ancient blue Caduceus. The giant trees in Lordran are distant offspring of the great stone archtrees. This shield inherits their properites, and the wood greatly reduces magic damage."
location: [1] - On a corpse in the graveyard next to Firelink Shrine. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 1:46.

description: Round wooden shield. Cracked and nearly broken. Wood shields are lighter than metal shields, but this one has lower physical damage reduction stability, partly due to cracking."
location: [1] - Initial equipment for Pyromancer. [2] - Dropped by Hollow Warriors (with shield) in the Firelink Shrine, Undead Burg, and Undead Parish.

description: Weapon born from the mystical creature of the Darkroot Garden, the Moonlight Butterfly. Imbued with a powerful crystal power, like the butterfly created by Seath the Scaleless. Can emit crystal light rings
location: [1] - Ascend: From the Soul of the Moonlight Butterfly and a +10 shield by the Giant Blacksmith.

description: "Frightful occult shield. Defends against divine weapons and lightning. In an ill-fated plot to destroy the very gods, the followers of the occult once attempted to steal the power of Gravelord Nito, the first of the dead."
location: [1] - Found in Tomb of Giants, on a path close to the second bonfire. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 17:12.

description: "Round leather-covered shield. A standard, widely-used shield. Small shields are always less stable, but landing critical hits after parry is easier."
location: [1] - Purchase from the Undead Merchant for 800 Souls. [2] - Initial equipment for Wanderer.

description: "Makeshift shield built from wood planks. Provides minimal protection, but at a cost of moderate humiliation."
location: [1] - Found on a corpse in Blighttown, just before the hidden entrance to The Great Hollow. [2] - Starter Item: Initial Equipment for Deprived. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 0:05.

description: "Round wooden shield crafted in Lordran featuring an impressive red and white design. The giant trees in Lordran are distant offspring of the great stone archtrees. This shield inherits their properties, and the wood greatly reduces magic damage."
location: [1] - Drops from skeleton with shield (2% chance - Firelink Shrine Graveyard and The Catacombs).

description: Small, leather-covered round shield, reinforced in critical spots with metal.Small shields are always less stable, but landing critical hits after parry is easier."
location: [1] - Purchase from the Undead Merchant for 600 Souls. [2] - Initial equipment for Sorcerer. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 1:02.

description: Small round metal shield. Four protrusions used to parry attacks. Small shields are always less stable, but landing critical hits after parry is easier. This shield is specialized for parrying.
location: [1] - Found on a body inside a small room in Lower Undead Burg just before the Capra Demon. [2] - Initial Equipment for Thief. [3] - Drops from Undead Assassin also known as Hollow Thief (1% chance - Lower Undead Burg). [4] - Video Location: Found in this video at 3:04.

description: "Round wooden shield crafted in Lordran. Decorated with the ancient Labrys axe. The giant trees in Lordran are distant offspring of the great stone archtrees. This shield inherits their properties, and the wood reduces magic damage."
location: [1] - Purchase from the Undead Merchant for 800 souls.

description: "Shield of the knights of the ancient kingdom of Balder. Tweaked for improved stability. Balder was the home of Knight King Rendal, but the kingdom was reduced to ruins after a widespread outbreak of Undead"
location: [1] - Rare drop (1% chance) from Balder Knights who wield it in Undead Parish and Sen's Fortress (they also wield the Balder Side Sword). [2] - Purchase: Crestfallen Merchant for 4000 souls.

description: Shield of the Black Knights that wander Lordran. A flowing canal is chiseled deeply into its face. Long ago, the black knights faced the chaos demons, and were charred black, but their shields became highly resistant to fire.
location: [1] - Drops from The Black Knight. (5% chance - Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Darkroot Basin, The Catacombs, Northern Undead Asylum, Tomb of Giants and Kiln of the First Flame).

description: "The bloodshield spoken of in the lost legends. The red blood is slightly enchanted, and boost various resistances."
location: [1] - Location: In the Painted World of Ariamis on the bridge near the Undead Dragon. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 5:53.

description: "Medium metal shield. Decorated by an ancient blue Caduceus. A standard, widely-used shield."
location: [1] - Purchase: Andre of Astora for 1000 souls. [2] - Drops from Swordsman Undead Crystal Soldier (2% chance - The Duke's Archives).

description: Shield of a nameless knight, likely a high-ranking knight of Astora." "One of the enchanted blue shields. The Crest Shield greatly reduces magic damage."
location: [1] - Drops from Oscar of Astora. (Guaranteed - Northern Undead Asylum Revisit).

description: "A fully crystallized straight shield. How such a weapon was created is entirely unknown." "The crystallization boosts its defense, but makes the shield brittle. The shield cannot be used for long, as it cannot be repaired."
location: [1] - Purchase: Domhnall of Zena for 4000 souls.

description: Shield of a nameless knight, likely a high-ranked knight of Astora. One of the enchanted blue shields. The dragon crest shield greatly reduces fire damage.
location: [1] - Valley of Drakes, right under the nose of a dozing Zombie Dragon (the middle of the three items it guards). [2] - Video Location Timestamp 1:16.

description: "A wooden kite shaped shield. It is decorated with the ancient symbol of a double-headed eagle, painted yellow." "Wooden shields are lighter than metal shields, but with lower physical damage reduction, and reduced shield stability."
location: [1] - Starter Item: Initial equipment for Cleric. [2] - Purchase: Undead Merchant (male) in Undead Burg for 400 souls.

description: "Bronze shield of the gargoyle guarding the Bell of Awakening in the Undead Church." "The gargoyle's skin is tough by itself, and perhaps its shield may have been a prop, for it is a rare example of a metal shield that does not reduce physical damage 100%."
location: [1] - Rare drop from any of the three Gargoyles with shields. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 9:24.

description: "Old medium metal shield of unknown origin. The grass crest is lightly imbued with magic, which slightly speeds stamina recovery."
location: [1] - Darkroot Basin, at the bottom of Darkroot Garden guarded by a Black Knight. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 12:53.

description: "Small metal shield. A standard, widely-used shield." "This shield is easy to use, and is one of the smallest shields that offers 100% physical damage reduction."
location: [1] - Starter Item: Initial Equipment for Warrior. [2] - Purchase: Undead Merchant for 1000 Souls.

description: "Metal shield wielded by Hollow soldiers. Heavily rusted but sturdy, providing a strong defense"
location: [1] - Obtained as a rare drop from Undead Parish soldiers. [2] - Drops from Undead Soldiers wielding a shield (1% Chance - Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Northern Undead Asylum (Revisit), Firelink Shrine).

description: "Thick iron shield of Shiva of the East. Heaviest of the standard shields. Deflects enemy attacks. A famous specimen from Shiva's collection, but none who have faced it have lived to tell the tale."
location: [1] - Drop from Shiva of the East.

description: "This medium metal shield is elegantly carved and painted." "Although it is thought to have been used by ancient knights of nobility, its defense and capabilities are similar to other shields."
location: [1] - On a corpse in the Undead Parish just past the Fog Gate. Guarded by an Balder Knight. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 4:33. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 4:30. [4] - Crystal Knight Shield can be found in one of the Mimics at the Duke's Archive, at the upper floor of the library.

description: "Large, leather-covered round shield. Choice shield for hunters." "Wood shields are lighter than metal shields, but with lower physical damage reductions, and reduced shield stability."
location: [1] - Starter Item: Initial equipment for Hunter. [2] - Purchase: Andre of Astora for 800 souls. [3] - Drops from Phalanx (1% Chance - Painted World of Ariamis).

description: "Shield of certain knights of Catarina, used in attacks with a large protrusion in the center." "Can be used as a thrust weapon."
location: [1] - Gift: Find Siegmeyer of Catarina in the Blighttown swamp near a group of slugs and give him 3 Purple Moss Clumps.

description: "A legendary weapon of the Way of the White, granted to an Undead paladin long ago" "Once blessed with the protection of a white flame, but its power has all but faded, so that it provides only slight HP recovery."
location: [1] - Defeating Paladin Leeroy in the Tomb of the Giants before killing the area boss.

description: "Shield of the Silver Knights of Anor Londo. A flowing canal is carved deep into its face. The Silver Knights stayed behind in Anor Londo to defend the illusory goddess, and their shields are blessed with her divine protection."
location: [1] - Obtained as a rare drop from Silver Knights in Anor Londo. [2] - Drops from Silver Knight (1% Chance - Anor Londo).

description: "Shield of the savage mountain bandits. Uniquely-shaped with a large black spider etched upon it." "Has resistance to poison."
location: [1] - Starter Item: Initial Equipment for Bandit. [2] - Locations: In the Valley of Drakes on the bridge to New Londo Ruins Lower on a corpse, and above the Giant Rat in the Depths by dropping down the hole behind the first Butcher. Video Location Timestamp 9:49.

description: "Shield of the infamous Darkwraith Kirk, Knight of Thorns. Covered with spikes." "Can be used as a weapon. Sharp spikes cause heavy bleeding."
location: [1] - Possible drop from any of the three encounters with Knight Kirk.

description: "Shield of Solaire of Astora, Knight of Sunlight. Decorated with a holy symbol, but Solaire illustrated it himself, and it has no divine powers of its own. As it turns out, Solaire's incredible prowess is a product of his own training, and nothing else."
location: [1] - Drops from Solaire. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 13:35.

description: "Medium metal shield. Decorated with a tower, the symbol of protection. A standard, widely-used shield."
location: [1] - Starter Item: Initial Equipment for Knight. [2] - Purchase: Andre of Astora for 1000 souls.

description: "Vertically elongated wooden shield. Decorated with a white dragon. "Wood shields are lighter than metal shields, but with lower physical damage reduction, and reduced shield stability."
location: [1] - On a corpse behind a door just before the first bonfire in the Undead Burg. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 1:50.

description: Standard battle axe. Inflicts regular damage, making it effective in various situations." "Powerful attack due to its weight, but one wrong swing leaves the wielder wide open, so timing and proximity to the enemy must be judged carefully."
location: [1] - Starting equipment for Bandit. [2] - Sold by Andre of Astora for 1,000 souls. [3] - Rare drop from axe-wielding Hollow Warriors.

description: Giant butcher knife wielded by the Undead man-eating cook lurking in the Depths. More of a tool for subduing and preparing live catches than an actual weapon. Those who have faced this deadly blade have a deeper sense of how helpless prey must feel."
location: [1] - Black Phantom Maneater Mildred.

description: Well-used old bronze battle axe with a long hilt and a crescent-shaped blade." "One of the blessed weapons of the Way of White. The Crescent Axe is bequeathed to cleric warriors who have proven their faith."
location: [1] - Sold by Patches the Hyena at Firelink Shrine for 10,000 souls. [2] - Dropped by Patches the Hyena.

description: Sliced tail of the gargoyle guarding the Bell of Awakening in the Undead Church or patrolling in Anor Londo. Can be used as a bronze battle axe. Bends dramatically during large attacks, owing to its nature as a tail."
location: [1] - Give to Frampt to receive 50 souls. [2] - Special: This axe increases Bleeding, Poison and Toxin resistance x2. (inventory screen = x4, but equipped = x2) causing the illusion that you get poisoned and bled when you equip it. [3] - Its shaft also bends on heavy attacks. [4] - If your attack doesn't connect with anything, there will be a slight lag at the end of the attack, similar to halberd weapons. [5] - Upgrading the weapon does not increase its Bleed, Poison, and Toxin resistance. The reason the player sees the Bleed, Poison, and Toxin meters when upgrading is because you technically are unequipping and re-equipping the weapon each time you upgrade. [6] - Poison and Toxin resistance make this weapon a good choice in Blighttown.

description: An axe born from the soul of the Iron Golem, guardian of Sen's Fortress who repelled countless heroes who sought Anor Londo." "Its powerful one-handed attack imitates the Golem's blade of wind, but the heavy iron blade makes it quite unwieldy."
location: [1] - Ascending an axe weapon+10 with the Core of an Iron Golem At the Giant Blacksmith in Anor Londo.

description: Small hand axe. Appears identical to a lumberjack's tool, but has an ideal weight and strength, and is easy to handle. One wrong swing leaves the wielder wide open, so timing and proximity to the enemy must be judged carefully."
location: [1] - Undead Merchant (Male) in Undead Burg for 450 souls. [2] - Obtained as starting equipment for pyromancers.

description: The preferred black bow of the heroic archer Pharis. Has a longer range than standard bows, but is more difficult to use. Without proper abilities, results will be underwhelming."
location: [1] - Darkroot Garden. Drops from Pharis, a faded, female hunter, who stands far to the right of the entrance of the area where Alvina sits, along the cliffside.

description: Composite bow emphasizing power. Requires more strength than standard bows. However, its range is shorter, making it unfit for sniping.
location: [1] - New Londo Ruins, on a ledge above the large gates overlooking Valley of Drakes. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 4:58.

description: Bow born from the soul of the Dark Sun Gwyndolin, Darkmoon deity who watches over the abandoned city of the Gods, Anor Londo. This golden bow is imbued with powerful magic and is most impressive with Moonlight Arrows."
location: [1] - Upgrade a +10 bow with Dark Sun Gwyndolin's soul.

description: Large bow. Projectile weapon for experienced hunters.
location: [1] - A ledge in Darkroot Garden, on the path leading down to Darkroot Basin. The corpse it is found on also contains the Hunter armor and some arrows. [2] - Drops from Undead Crystal Archer (2% chance - the Duke's Archives).

description: Small bow. Standard projectile weapon." Equip arrows to use. Hold bow and press L1 or LB to aim. Aim. for heads of humanoid foes. Press L2/R2 or LT/R/T to change type of arrows.
location: [1] - Initial equipment for Hunter. [2] - Undead Merchant (male) in Undead Burg sell the weapon for 1000 Souls. [3] - Drops from Hollows (2% chance - Undead Burg, Northern Undead Asylum, Depths, Northern Undead Asylum (upon returning) and Painted World of Ariamis). [4] - Drops from Torch Hollows (2% chance - Northern Undead Asylum, Lower Undead Burg, Depths and Painted World of Ariamis). [5] - Drops from skeleton (2% chance - Firelink Shrine Graveyard and The Catacombs).

description: Catalyst belonging to Beatrice, the rogue witch. Contrasts with Vinheim catalysts. This ancient catalyst shows signs of being used for age-old sorceries. It has passed the hands of many generations to get here."
location: [1] - At Valley of Drakes area along with the Witch Armor Set. Basically go where the cave entrance to Blighttown is; the corpse is located at a ledge on at the opposite side of the cave entrance. [2] - Available only after you have defeated Four Kings boss. [3] - It is not necessary to have summoned Witch Beatrice. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 0:10.

description: Demon catalyst formed from Izalith molten rock. Can be used as fire weapon. The Demon Firesage was the first demon, and the last master of the original fire arts, before the Witch of Izalith was engulfed by Chaos, creating pyromancy."
location: [1] - Drops from Demon Firesage in Demon Ruins.

description: Catalyst of the Witch of Izalith of long ago, when her daughters were still flame witches, before they were engulfed by the Chaos Flame. Before the birth of pyromancy, their wands were mediums for sorcery, but knowledge of this flame sorcery has long since vanished."
location: [1] - Drop from the Daughter of Chaos enemy in Lost Izalith, in front of the Bed of Chaos boss door. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 15:54.

description: Catalyst of Big Hat Logan, the great sorcerer and seeker of knowledge. Originally the same catalyst employed by the Vinheim sorcerers, only terribly strengthened over time due to Logan's use. Very powerful when used by one of superior intelligence."
location: [1] - The Duke's Archives/Crystal Cave. After buying all Big Hat Logan's spells in the Archives, and defeating Seath the Scaleless, talk to him once more, then reload the area. Logan will no longer be there (having gone hollow and moved to Seath's boss room), and a chest with his staff and armor will have appeared in his place. (Note: you must purchase all his spells, even those you already have, for him to leave.).

description: A sorcery catalyst born from the soul of Manus, Father of the Abyss. A rough, old wooden catalyst large enough to be used as a strike weapon. Similar to the Tin Crystallization Catalyst, it boosts the strength of sorceries, but limits the number of castings. "
location: [1] - Ascend: From any catalyst, along with Soul of Manus, at the Giant Blacksmith in Anor Londo.

description: Sorcery catalyst of the lost land of Oolacile. Formed by enchanted whitebark branches. Boosts sorcery adjustment, but lost land sorceries are not offensive, and attacks are not affected by intelligence."
location: [1] - Drops from Oolacile Sorceress (0.5% Chance - Oolacile Township and Chasm of the Abyss).

description: Sorcery catalyst of lost land of Oolacile. Formed by enchanted whitebark branches. The whitebark boosts sorcery adjustment, but the sorceries of this lost land are gentle and not affected by intelligence."
location: [1] - Sold by Dusk of Oolacile in Darkroot Basin. After defeating the Hydra, find a cave at the back of the lake and kill the gold crystal golem there to free Dusk. Head back to the other side of the lake and summon her from her soul sign to buy from her.

description: Sorcery catalyst used by sorcerers of Vinheim Dragon School. Equip catalyst to use sorceries. Attune sorceries from a scroll at a bonfire. Most sorceries have a limited number of uses."
location: [1] - Starting equipment for Sorcerer. [2] - Found on a corpse in the same room Griggs of Vinheim can be rescued in. [3] - Sold by Griggs of Vinheim after rescuing him from lower Undead Burg. Video Location: Found in this video at 2:21. [4] - Sold by Blacksmith Rickert of Vinheim in New Londo Ruins. [5] - Video Location Timestamp 2:12.

description: Catalyst of the banishment sorcerers who flooded New Londo to seal off away the Darkwraiths and the Kings who fell to Dark. In contrast to other catalysts, the metal catalyst qualifies as a weapon, as it inflicts physical damage, however small."
location: [1] - Loot from dead body in Blighttown along with Sealer's Armor Set (above the elevator down to the swamp, across a tree root).

description: Catalyst imbued in terrifying crystal magic. Used by Logan after his fixation on Seath. Makes all sorceries incredibly powerful, but demands extremely high intelligence from its wielder, and halves sorcery usages. "
location: [1] - Drop: Big Hat Logan (mad). After purchasing all the spells from Logan at The Duke's Archives and defeating Seath the Scaleless, talk to him once more then leave the area or quit/ load game. Return to the room where you fought Seath the Scaleless the first time and you will find him there, defeat him to get his drop. (Note: be sure to purchase all his spells for him to go mad, even the ones you already have.). [2] - Alternatively, the catalyst can be obtained before defeating Seath the Scaleless by killing passive Big Hat Logan in The Duke's Archives after buying all of his spells. This will prevent the player from obtaining Logan's Catalyst and the remainder of the Big Hat's Set as killing him at this point fails the questline.

description: Catalyst born from the soul of the Dark Sun Gwyndolin, Darkmoon deity who watches over the abandoned city of the Gods, Anor Londo. Gwyndolin is Gwyn's lastborn and a legitimate god, but he is also a Moon sorcerer, and this wand is boosted by faith, not intelligence."
location: [1] - Ascend from any other Catalyst except Demon's Catalyst, Izalith Catalyst, and Manus Catalyst for 5,000 Souls, along with Dark Sun Gwyndolin's soul at the Giant Blacksmith in Anor Londo.

description: Repeating crossbow cherished by the weapon craftsman Eidas. Its elaborate design makes it closer to a work of art than a weapon. Intricate mechanism makes heavy damage possible through triple-shot firing of bolts. but in fact each bolt inflicts less damage."
location: [1] - Give to Frampt to receive 50 souls. [2] - Special: Avelyn fires 3 bolts in quick succession, all of which deal the weapon's full damage. [3] - After you open the chest, if you have not cleared out the enemies in the room prior to attempting to get the chest, it is strongly advised to warp back to a bonfire instead of attempting to clear the room. Immediately upon opening the chest, you will be pelted by arrows from the undead archers and spells from the channeler, on top of having already taken heavy damage from the fall. If you fall off the bookshelf to the right, it should give you enough time to warp back to a bonfire. The reason for this is if you happen to die after getting the chest, there is a possibility that your souls will spawn on top of the bookshelf, even if you died on ground level. And you really don't want to have to get back onto that bookshelf again, especially if your bloodstain holds a lot of Humanity.

description: Powerful large crossbow. While bows require both hands, a crossbow is held in one, but arming each bolt takes time."
location: [1] - Area just before Gaping Dragon. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 16:28.

description: Standard crossbow issued to soldiers. While bows require both hands, a crossbow is held in one, but arming each bolt takes time. "
location: [1] - It can be found on a corpse in the Upper Undead Burg, along with 16 Standard Bolts. It is located on the balcony of one of the buildings. It can only be reached by jumping a gap from an adjacent building, most easily accessible from the roof, that is first entered via a fog door. [2] - Drops from Undead Knight Archer (2% - Sen's Fortress). [3] - Drops from Balder Knight with crossbow (2% chance - Sen's Fortress and Undead Parish). [4] - Drops from Undead Soldier wielding a crossbow (2% Chance - Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Northern Undead Asylum (Revisit), Firelink Shrine). [5] - Video Location Timestamp 5:15.

description: Large crossbow with long distance used by Carim snipers. Often used with sniper bolts. But while crossbows are normally easy to use, the Sniper Crossbow requires dexterity."
location: [1] - Sen's Fortress. Close to bonfire, right below the broken bridge. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 22:25.

description: Sword wielded only by servants of Gravelord Nito, the first of the dead. Crafted from the bones of the fallen.""The miasma of death exudes from the sword, a veritable toxin to any living being."
location: [1] - Join the Gravelord Servant Covenant. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 3:47.

description: Giant curved sword forged using special methods in an Eastern Land. This unparalleled weapon cuts like a Katana but is heavier than a Nata machete. Requires extreme strength, dexterity, and stamina to wield."
location: [1] - Killing Shiva of the East. [2] - Rare drop from the Giant Skeletons in Firelink Shrine and the Tomb of the Giants.

description: A curved greatsword used in a mysterious ancient rite. Imbued with a frightful occult energy which restores HP with each hit."
location: [1] - Poisoned swamp at the bottom of Blighttown, in the leeches nest. Video Location Timestamp 19:52.

description: Small, curved sword. Each hit inflicts little damage, but fluid chain attacks are deadly.The falchion's sharp slashing attacks are effective against cloth and flesh, but not against metal armor or tough scales."
location: [1] - Blighttown treasure, found along with Wanderer clothes. [2] - Dropped by skeletons (Falchion) in The Catacombs and at Firelink Shrine. [3] - An Enchanted version drops from a Mimic in the The Duke's Archives. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 31:38.

description: Curved sword used by the Lord's Blade Ciaran, one of Gwyn's Four Knights. Ciaran brandishes her sword in a mesmerizing dance, etching the darkness with dire streaks of gold."
location: [1] - Acquired from Lord's Blade Ciaran alongside the Dark Silver Tracer, in exchange for the Soul of Artorias, or by killing her (you can't acquire the weapons twice). Ciaran can be found in the same place you encounter Knight Artorias after you defeat him, and will ask for his Soul. Refusing will not make her hostile. Killing her without trading the soul will make her drop both the tracers and her armor set. (Causing her to appear may require dying or warping somewhere after defeating Artorias).

description: Jagged blade wielded by New Londo ghosts. Violent thrust attack." "One of the cursed weapons. Inflicts damage to ghosts."
location: [1] - Dropped by dual blade ghost (1% chance - New Londo Ruins).

description: Curved sword of Anor Londo painting guards. Unique shape with flat tip.""The guardians, who strike down those who dare threaten the paintings, attack in a continuous, circular dancing motion, a technique passed down through generations."
location: [1] - Drops from Painting Guardians (2% Chance - Anor Londo).

description: A curved sword born from the soul of Quelaag, daughter of the Witch of Izalith, who was transformed into a chaos demon. Like Quelaag's body, the sword features shells, spikes, humanity and a coating of chaos fire. Power affected by wielder's humanity."
location: [1] - Give to Frampt to receive 5000 souls. [2] - Special: This sword also gets additional damage from your held humanity, max at 10 humanity. [3] - The kick action (forward + light attack) is replaced by a quick horizontal slash followed by a backward semi-cartweel motion. If the weapon is held in the left hand it can be used to parry and attack. It is also able to block when 2-handed. [4] - This sword is somewhat slower than the other curved swords, but the difference is barely noticeable. [5] - If you have fully grown parasite, you will have parasite kick animation.

description: Small, curved sword. Each hit inflicts little damage, but fluid chain attacks are deadly." "The scimitar's sharp slashing attacks are effective against cloth and flesh, but not against metal armor or tough scales."
location: [1] - Starting weapon for Wanderer. [2] - Sold by the Undead Merchant in the Undead Burg for 600 souls. [3] - Drops from skeleton with scimitar & shield (2% chance - Firelink Shrine Graveyard and The Catacombs).

description: Curved sword with sharply curved blade.""Created by Arstor, Earl of Carim. Requires great skill to wield, but evades shield defense to sneak in damage."
location: [1] - Sen's Fortress - On the ledge behind Siegmeyer at the first rolling boulder area. [2] - Sold by Shiva of the East for 10,000 souls. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 7:09.

description: "This wide single-edged shortsword is the favorite of lowly thieves and bandits.""Primarily a slicing weapon, but highly effective when used for critical hits, such as after parrying or from behind."
location: [1] - Default equipment for the Thief class. [2] - Drops from Undead Assassin (2% chance - Lower Undead Burg).

description: This standard small dagger has only a modest attack but can be jabbed in rapid succession, and is effective in critical hits such as after a parry or when stabbing in the back." "With both slash and thrust attacks this dagger is useful in various situations."
location: [1] - Default equipment for the Sorcerer class. [2] - Purchased from Undead Merchant (male) (300 Souls). [3] - Video Location Timestamp 1:11.

description: A dark silver dagger used by the Lord's Blade Ciaran, of Gwyn's Four Knights. The victim is first distracted by dazzling streaks of the Gold Tracer, then stung by the vicious poison of this dagger."
location: [1] - Acquired from Lord's Blade Ciaran alongside the Gold Tracer, in exchange for the Soul of Artorias, or by killing her (you can't acquire the daggers twice in one run). Ciaran can be found in the same place you encounter Knight Artorias, after you defeat him, and will ask for his Soul. Refusing will not make her hostile. Killing her causes her to drop both the tracers and her armor as loot if you have not traded the soul. (Causing her to appear may require you to reload or warp somewhere else after defeating Artorias). [2] - It is important to note that if the player speaks to Ciaran and refuses to give her the soul, she will disappear from your playthrough forever when next you reload the area.

description: The ghosts of New Londo clutch this wickedly sharp dagger with their bony hands and make mincemeat of the living." "One of the cursed weapons which can inflict damage upon ghosts."
location: [1] - Drops from Banshees (1% chance - New Londo Ruins). It does NOT drop from other ghosts, they drop Jagged Ghost Blades instead.

description: This dagger is useful after parrying and is normally equipped in the left hand.""A favorite of the knights of Carim, who are famous for fighting without a shield."
location: [1] - Can be found on a corpse in upper New Londo Ruins, in the same area as the first Banshee. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 2:38.

description: "This sword, one of the rare dragon weapons, came from the tail of Priscilla, the Dragon Crossbreed in the painted world of Ariamis.""Possessing the power of lifehunt, it dances about when wielded, in a fashion reminiscent of the white-robed painting guardians."
location: [1] - Obtained by cutting off Crossbreed Priscilla's Tail. [2] - Before the battle begins, while Priscilla is standing there (no boss health bar at the bottom of the screen), position yourself near her tail. Hit her TAIL as a start to the fight. Keep swinging as she turns invisible because she won't attack you until she completely vanishes. After about 1/4th of her health bar drops, the tail should come right off.

description: The weapon augments one's bare hands with thick, studded leather. The Caestus has a short reach, but quick cool down. Amount of damage inflicted is dependent on its wearer's strength."
location: [1] - Purchased from Andre of Astora for 200 souls.

description: A weapon formed by three sharped claws. Attacks cause bleeding, and the wounds inflicted are not easily mended. Preferred weapon of the spooks of an Eastern land.
location: [1] - Sold by Shiva of the East for 5,000 souls.

description: The Darkwraiths, incited by Kaathe, use the power of the dark soul to absorb humanity, an art shared by this weapon, which also acts as a special shield. The ancients, particularly, could sap the humanity of even a replete saint in the blink of an eye."
location: [1] - Joining the Darkwraith Covenant. [2] - Drops from Darkwraith Knights (1% Chance - New Londo Ruins).

description: A weapon from the soul of the Iron Golem, guardian of Sen's Fortress who repelled countless heroes who sought Anor Londo." "The Gods fused the power of the soul with the great bones of the dragons, forming an appropriate core for the giant golem'"
location: [1] - Ascending Caestus or Claws +10 with the Core of an Iron Golem at the Giant Blacksmith in Anor Londo. [2] - Does not ascend with Dark Hand.

description: Flame medium used by Great Swamp pyromancers. Pyromancers arouse this flame to produce various fire arts. Equip pyromancer flame to produce pyromancy. Attune pyromancies from a scroll at bonfire. Most pyromancies have limited number of uses." After upgrading: "Pyromancy flame drawing upon the latent power of Quelana of Izalith, mother of pyromancy. For the pyromancer, a flame is a precious thing indeed, to be nurtured, often for an entire lifetime. When the flame is shared, it creates an eternal bond between the parties."
location: [1] - Starting equipment for the Pyromancer. [2] - Obtained by other classes after rescuing Laurentius of the Great Swamp from the Depths. [3] - Also obtainable for all classes through the "Talk" option from Eingyi after having spoken to him while infected with egghead. If you have less than 11 Int Eingyi will not offer you the flame.

description: Greataxe of the Black Knights who wander Lordran. Used to face chaos demons." "The large motion that puts the weight of the body into the attack reflects the great size of their adversaries long ago."
location: [1] - Black Knight (Axe) (The Catacombs) (does not respawn). [2] - Black Knight (Axe) (Kiln of The First Flame) (respawns).

description: Carved from the bones of fellow demons. Wielded by the lesser Taurus demons." "This axe is imbued with no special power, but can merrily beat foes to a pulp, providing you have the strength to wield it."
location: [1] - Dropped by Taurus Demons. [2] - Sold by Shiva of the East for 10,000 Souls. [3] - Drops from Minor Taurus Demon (5% chance - Demon Ruins).

description: This axe, one of the rare dragon weapons, is formed by the tail of the Gaping Dragon, a distant, deformed descendant of the everlasting dragons." "The axe is imbued with a mystical power, to be released when held with both hands."
location: [1] - Remove the Gaping Dragon's tail.

description: This greataxe is a veritable mass of iron. Its weight sends foes flying, but makes it difficult to handle without inhuman strength." "Any miss makes one very vulnerable, as each swing requires the full force of the body."
location: [1] - Can be found in the Depths (in a box behind 5 rats near the bonfire). [2] - Sold by Crestfallen Merchant for 8000 souls. [3] - Rare drop from the Berenike Knights. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 5:36.

description: Greataxe borne by the stone knights who guard the forest Sanctuary. This greataxe, requiring inhuman strength to wield and more fitted to manual labor than battle, is nevertheless completely lethal."
location: [1] - Drops from Stone Guardian (2% chance - Royal Wood).

description: Demon weapon built from the stone archtrees. Used by lesser demons at North Undead Asylum. This hammer is imbued with no special power, but will merrily beat foes to a pulp, provided you have the strength to wield it."
location: [1] - Beat the Asylum Demon during the first encounter. Only drops it during the very first encounter, before going through the rest of the tutorial area and dropping back in after you pick up your weapons. Easy strategy: Choose Black Firebombs as your gift - no need to be naked or only use your fists. Alternatively: Wait until NG+ and beat him easily with your proper gear. [2] - Trade a Sack with Snuggly.

description: Created from an everlasting dragon tooth. Legendary great hammer of Havel the Rock." "The dragon tooth will never break as it is harder than stone, and it grants its wielder resistance to magic and flame."
location: [1] - Chest behind a hidden wall in Anor Londo. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 15:37.

description: A legendary weapon of the Way of White, granted to an Undead paladin long ago. This mass of iron can only be lifted by those with inhuman strength, but it is blessed, and very effective against agents of Dark."
location: [1] - Give to Frampt to receive 100 souls. [2] - The 2-handed strong attack of this hammer generates an AOE attack similar to the miracle Wrath of Gods, but with more range, damage and 100% chance of knockback (even against high poise targets, excluding bosses). Damage from this ability scales with Faith and strength. The AOE does exactly the same amount of damage as a two-handed jump attack. Except at 200 durability or less the AOE attack does the same amount of damage as the two-handed jump attack with 0 durability. Between 380 and 201 durability the two-handed jump attack still does full damage even though the AOE attack does not. The divine modifier also applies to the AOE attack, so unlike Wrath of the Gods it can be used to kill skeletons. Each use of this ability drains 200 durability. When using the AOE between 201 and 380 durability it will do about 3/5 of the maximum damage potential (depending of your stat distribution). When using the AOE at 200 durability or less, it will do around 1/3 of the maximum damage potential (depending on your stat distribution). If you try to use the ability at 0/600 durability it will not work at all, however, your character will still do the animation and be vulnerable to attack.

description: Giant tree branch serves as a wooden club. Smashes enemies from upside the head." "The leaping attack is a trademark of clubs, and this giant club is no exception."
location: [1] - Found in Blighttown near Quelaag's Domain. Pocket island with Rock-Tosser Barbarians. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 17:22.

description: Large wooden club." "The giant Undead of Blighttown wield these clubs, which became poisonous from the influence of their surroundings."
location: [1] - Drops from club wielder Infested Barbarian (2% Chance - Blighttown).

description: Great Hammer from the soul of executioner Smough, who guards the cathedral in the forsaken city of Anor Londo. Smough loved his work, and ground the bones of his victims into his own feed, ruining his hopes of being ranked with the Four Knights."
location: [1] - Give to Frampt to receive 1 souls. [2] - This weapon will heal 10 HP with every successful hit. [3] - The sheer size of the head tends to get in the way, limiting your field of vision. [4] - This weapon has a very slightly larger hitbox on the 2-handed rolling attacks when compared to other Great Hammers. While the increased range on this specific attack is quite minor, when paired with the fact that rolling attacks from Great Hammers cannot be parried, this can be quite useful in PVP. [5] - Two-handed R2 attacks cause Knockdown. [6] - To get the Soul of Smough be sure to kill orenstein first as you need to win against super smough to get his soul.

description: "Bow of the Dragonslayers, led by Hawkeye Gough, one of Gwyn's Four Knights. This bow's unusual size requires that it be anchored to the ground when fired. Only uses specialized great arrows."
location: [1] - Give to Frampt to receive 100 souls. [2] - Even though this weapon is two-handed, it will still require 20 strength to use. [3] - Can only be used with Dragonslayer Arrows and Gough's Great Arrows.

description: Greatbow used by Hawkeye Gough, of Lord Gwyn's Four Knights, for dragon slaying. This bow is larger than even those used by the famed Dragonslayers. Only their leader, Gough, had the strength to handle it."
location: [1] - After defeating Black Dragon Kalameet, talk to Gough once more and he will give you his bow. You may have to use the talk option many times before he gives the bow to you. [2] - Also dropped by Gough when killed, unless he already gave it to you.

description: This greatsword belonged to Lord Gwyn's Knight Artorias, who fell to the Abyss. Swallowed by the Dark with its master, this sword is tainted by the Abyss, and now its strength reflects its wielder's humanity."
location: [1] - Ascension with the Soul of Artorias and any normal +10 Greatsword, Straight Sword or Dagger (except Broken Straight Sword and Straight Sword Hilt).

description: This standard greatsword is normally wielded with two hands due to its great weight." "Usually swung in large arcs and effective against multiple foes. Far from ideal when fighting in tight quarters.
location: [1] - Purchased from Blacksmith Andre (3,000 Souls).

description: "Greatsword of the Black Knights who wander Lordran. Used to face chaos demons.""The Large motion that puts the weight of the body into the attack reflects the great size of their adversaries long ago."
location: NA

description: This larger type of greatsword is normally wielded with two hands due to its weight.""This highly flexible greatsword can be used to attack in swings or thrusts."
location: [1] - Looted from a body on the bridge, guarded by the Hellkite Wyvern, in upper Undead Burg. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 1:27.

description: A fully crystallized straight sword. How such a weapon was created is entirely unknown." "The crystallization boosts its attack, but makes the blade brittle. The sword cannot be used for long, as it cannot be repaired."
location: [1] - Purchased from Domhnall of Zena (6,000 Souls).

description: This greatsword with an undulating blade is the choice weapon of the slithering serpent men of Sen's Fortress.""This weapon is designed to flay the skin and causes heavy bleeding."
location: [1] - Purchased from Shiva of the East (10,000 Souls). [2] - Rare drop from Serpent Mages (2% Chance - Sen's Fortress or The Duke's Archives).

description: "Greatsword born from the soul of Gwyn, Lord of Cinder.""As bearer of the ultimate soul, Gwyn wielded the bolts of the sun, but before linking the fire, divided that power amongst his children, and set off with only this greatsword as his companion."
location: [1] - Ascension with the Soul of Gwyn, Lord of Cinder and a +10 upgraded Dagger/ Straight Sword/ Great Sword. [2] - Compatible swords: Dagger, Parrying Dagger, Bandit's Knife, Shortsword, Longsword, Broadsword, Balder Side Sword, Sunlight Straight Sword, Barbed Straight Sword, Darksword, Bastard Sword, Claymore, Man-serpent Greatsword, Flamberge, Zweihander, Greatsword, Demon Great Machete, Straight Sword Hilt.

description: Sword born from the soul of the great grey wolf Sif, guardian of the grave of the Abysswalker Knight Artorias.""Sir Artorias hunted the Darkwraiths, and his sword strikes harder against dark servants."
location: [1] - Ascension with the Soul of (Great Wolf) Sif and either the Broken Straight Sword +10 or Straight Sword Hilt +10. [2] - In order to create the "Cursed" version of this sword (the variant that can damage Ghosts without applying Transient Curse), you can ascend the soul using any normal +10 upgraded Straight Sword. See Greatsword of Artorias (Cursed).

description: Sword born from the souls of the great grey wolf Sif, guardian of the grave of the Abysswalker Knight Artorias.""The sword can damage ghosts, as it was cursed when Artorias joined a covenant with the creatures of the Abyss."
location: [1] - Ascension with the Soul of (Great Wolf) Sif and any of the following normal +10 weapons: Dagger, Parrying Dagger, Bandit's Knife, Shortsword, Longsword, Broadsword, Balder Side Sword, Sunlight Straight Sword, Barbed Straight Sword, Darksword, Bastard Sword, Claymore, Man-serpent Greatsword, Flamberge, Zweihander, Greatsword, Demon Great Machete. [2] - In order to create the "Non-Cursed" version of this sword (the variant that has Divine and Abyss modifiers), you can ascend the soul using either the Broken Straight Sword +10 or Straight Sword Hilt +10. See Greatsword of Artorias.

description: "This single-bladed blood-stained greatsword is the choice weapon of the slithering serpent men of Sen's Fortress." "An ordinary human will have great difficulty swinging this heavy beast effectively."
location: [1] - Rare drop from Man Serpent (2% Chance - Sen's Fortress or The Duke's Archives).

description: This sword, one of the rare dragon weapons, came from the tail of Seath the Scaleless, the pale white dragon who betrayed his own.""Seath is the grandfather of sorcery, and this sword is imbued with his magic, which shall be unleashed as a wave of moonlight."
location: [1] - Acquired by cutting off Seath the Scaleless' tail. [2] - Out of the three tails, player must cut the middle one, by hitting the end of the tail until it comes off. Make sure you are lightweight enough to run quickly. Run over and stand by the crystal you need to destroy in order to damage the boss at the start of the fight, and wait for him to shuffle over and do a breath attack. Wait for his attack to end in a few seconds, then shatter the crystal which will stun him giving you plenty of time to chop or Soul Spear his tail off. The tail should fall off after about 500 damage. [3] - After patch 1.05, the sword gets directly transfered into your inventory, so it is no longer possibile to lose it for the playthrough, after cutting Seath's tail and leaving it's tip containing the weapon inside the boss arena. You will acquire the weapon, even without finishing off Seath.

description: This greataxe, one of the rare dragon weapons, is formed by the tail of the one-eyed black dragon Kalameet, the last of the ancient dragons. The mystical power of its obsidian blade will be released when held with both hands."
location: [1] - Obtained by cutting off the tail of Black Dragon Kalameet. This is difficult to do, as the opportunities to strike the tail without ranged weapons are short and infrequent without the help of phantoms.

description: The Stone Knights who guard the Darkroot Garden wield this moss-covered greatsword." "The same magic which created the Stone Knights is imbued in the sword. Unleash this power by wielding the sword with two hands."
location: [1] - Purchased from Shiva of the East (15,000 Souls). [2] - Drops from Great Stone Knights (2% chance - Darkroot Garden). [3] - Video Location Timestamp 4:29.

description: Halberd of the black knights who wander Lordran. Used to face chaos demons. The large motion that puts the weight of the body into the attack reflects the great sizeof their adversaries long ago.
location: [1] - Drops from The Black Knight, specifically the Halberd wielding Knight. (20% chance - Darkroot Basin, Tomb of Giants and Kiln of the First Flame).

description: Halberd of the gargoyle guarding the Bell of Awakening in the Undead Church." "Perfectly standard bronze halberd without any special power."
location: [1] - Random drop from either the Bell Gargoyles or the two Anor Londo Gargoyles. Can be farmed for by killing only one Bell Gargoyle and quitting the game/dying. [2] - A Gargoyle is located at Undead Parish. Anor Londo has two more Gargoyles. [3] - Video Location Timestamp 2:55.

description: Large halberd made of old brass, used by the giant sentinels of Anor Londo." "Brass contains traces of lightning, but as the halberd is designed for the giant sentinels, its weight is unbearable."
location: [1] - Drops from Sentinel (2% chance - Anor Londo). [2] - Drops from Royal Sentinel (2% chance - Anor Londo). [3] - Bought from Giant Blacksmith in Anor Londo. [4] - Give to Frampt to receive 50 souls.

description: Weapon with a long curved blade. Converted from a wheat-harvesting tool. The magnificent sharp curved blade instills fear in opponents. Perhaps it is their survival instinct at work."
location: NA

description: Long-hilted weapon mixing spear and axe is difficult to handle, requiring both strength and dexterity. The Halberd has two elementary attacks: Spear-like thrusting and large sweeping swings. However, one false swing and the wielder is left wide open."
location: [1] - Undead Parish, right in front of the Undead Church entrance. Additionally, a Crystal Halberd drops from a Mimic in Anor Londo. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 4:57.

description: Scythe born from the soul of Priscilla, the stark white crossbreed trapped inside the painted world of Ariamis. Even the Gods feared Priscilla's lifehunt ability, and in the hands of a mortal, its power will turn upon its wielder."
location: [1] - Forge from a +10 Scythe, Whip,or Halberd and Soul of Priscilla (+5000 souls) at the Giant Blacksmith in Anor Londo.

description: Halberd with a large, hard beak-like protrusion. Inflicts thrust damage. Instead of thrusting like a spear, the wielder of the Lucerne aims to club the head with its hard beak."
location: [1] - After the first small bridge in the Catacombs. In a small room guarded by two skeletons. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 0:32.

description: Halberd with a large blade. Scythe adjusted for battle. Designed especially for slicing. However, one false swing leaves one wide open.
location: [1] - Found at the bottom of Sen's Fortress, behind the Titanite Demon in the small room. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 1:55.

description: Weapon of the titanite demon, a faceless stone monster born from titanite slab. One of the enchanted weapons, perhaps from residual power of the titanite slab. Known for its leaping attack which comes smashing down on foes from above."
location: [1] - Rare (10%) drop from Titanite Demons. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 1:04.

description: Wooden hammer of the giant blacksmith in Anor Londo who handles lightning weapons. The giant blacksmith forges with this wooden hammer, as it would be hazardous to handle lightning weapons with metals.
location: [1] - Dropped by the Giant Blacksmith in Anor Londo.

description: Metal hammer of Andre of Astora, blacksmith at the Old Church. Can be used as a strike weapon, but better left in the hands of its talented owner."
location: [1] - Murdering Blacksmith Andre of Astora.

description: A simple wooden club. This simple bladeless strike weapon is effective against most foes, is easily handled, and can break the guard of a shield." "However, a single miss makes one wide open, so timing and proximities are crucial.
location: [1] - Sold by Undead Merchant (Male) for 150 Souls. [2] - Treasure: Occult Club drops from a Mimic in Anor Londo near Havel's gear. [3] - Deprived starting weapon. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 15:37.

description: Metal hammer of Vamos, a skeleton blacksmith deep within the Catacombs who rarely speaks. Can be used as a strike weapon, but better left in the hands of its talented owner.
location: [1] - Murdering Blacksmith Vamos.

description: Iron hammer with a protrusive pommel.This simple bladeless strike weapon is effective against most foes, and can break the guard of a shield. However, one miss leaves one wide open."
location: [1] - Starter Item for the Cleric class. [2] - Sold by Patches the Hyena at Firelink Shrine for 3,000 souls. [3] - In the Catacombs on a corpse before the Pinwheel boss fog. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 17:18.

description: Hammer with a sharp spike on its pommel. One of the more barbaric cleric weapons. Uniquely, this hammer inflicts thrust damage, and causes bleeding."
location: [1] - Firelink Shrine, in a chest found in the area you can reach by dropping from the ledge you find by following the stairs that is facing Petrus. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 0:56.

description: Traditionally a mining tool, but the hard cone on the pommel is effective in battle." "A downward swing of the Pickaxe can crush stone; a human head would be an afterthought"
location: [1] - Drops from Infested Barbarians (Boulder) in Blighttown (5% chance).

description: A club with leather nailed to the pommel. The burg Undead merchant proudly peddles this club, but its reinforcement is shoddy. Indeed, its attack is stronger, but the club is no longer dependably sturdy."
location: [1] - Sold by the Undead merchant (male).

description: Hammer with a large beak-shaped pommel. Originally a pickaxe; redesigned for battle." "This simple but deadly weapon hits heavily with thrust damage, such that it easily cracks metal armor."
location: [1] - Purchase from Andre of Astora in Undead Parish for 800 souls.

description: A curved sword born from the soul of Quelaag, daughter of the Witch of Izalith, who was transformed into a chaos demon. This blade inherits only the chaotic nature of Quelaag, and has a unique speckled design. Blade wielder erodes along with opponents."
location: [1] - 5,000 souls (each upgrade). [2] - 1 x Demon Titanite (+1, +2). [3] - 2 x Demon Titanite (+3, +4). [4] - 4 x Demon Titanite (+5).

description: Katana forged in an Eastern land. The Katana is drawn from the scabbard at lightning speed, using an Iaijutsu technique. Iaijutsu allows the wielder to charge forward to swiftly cut down distant foes."
location: [1] - Found by leaping to platform, close to the upper bonfire in Blighttown. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 5:14.

description: Katana forged in an Eastern land. Known for its brisk slashing motions." "The Uchigatana cuts beautifully and causes bleeding, but its blade is easily nicked."
location: NA

description: Katana forged in an Eastern land. Very unusual specimen with a long blade. Has a different move set than the Uchigatana. The blade is extremely long, but as a result, quite easily broken."
location: [1] - Can be bought from Shiva of the East for 20,000 Souls.

description: The largest of the thrusting swords. Can pierce an armored knight in one thrust.""The blade is also sharp, allowing slicing as well."
location: [1] - Corpse in the lower section of Firelink Shrine, along the path towards New Londo Ruins. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 1:11.

description: Standard small thrusting sword. Unsharpened but pointed solid shaft can pierce tough armour" "Can be thrust using the weight of one's body, and is very effective for critical attacks."
location: [1] - First room on the right where the first group of Undead Thieves come out in lower Undead Burg.

description: Standard thrusting sword. Regular one-handed attack can be delivered with shield held up. Can parry with use of left hand. Thrusting attacks pierce and are effective against foes with hard exteriors, but the wielder is left open to damage after a swing."
location: [1] - Drops from Balder Knight with Rapier (2% chance - Sen's Fortress and Undead Parish). [2] - Sold by Undead Merchant.

description: A rapier with intricate decorations. Chosen weapon of the famous Undead Prince Ricard. Ricard's exploits are told in a monomyth.""He was born into royalty, but wandered the lands in a fateful ill-conceived journey. He became Undead and disappeared up North."
location: [1] - Ricard the Archer on top of Sen's Fortress. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 23:45.

description: A symbolic, powerful thrusting sword used by the Pardoner serving Velka, Goddess of Sin." "It is no mere symbol to be sure; the Pardoner is an inhuman swordsman, and wields this enchanted blade with special sword technique."
location: [1] - In the Painted World of Ariamis, through the door requiring the Annex Key. After accessing the Annex using the Annex Key, continue upstairs. In the next section, there is a set of wooden planks on the right. Breaking them gives access to a corpse with the rapier on it. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 18:05.

description: Trident of the Six-eyed Channelers, sorcerers who serve Seath the Scaleless in collecting human specimens. Thrusted in circular motions in a unique martial arts dance that stirs nearby allies into a bloodthirsty frenzy.
location: [1] - Give to Frampt to receive 1 soul. [2] - Special: The two-handed heavy attack of the trident increases the attack power of allies by 20% this is a useful weapon before a boss fight when you have summoned Phantoms into your world or to give yourself some breathing room during an invasion. [3] - Does not affect the user. [4] - The duration of the 'channelers dance' buff lasts for 30 seconds. [5] - The durability also deteriorates fast (80 points per use) when using the special attack, and after three uses the weapon breaks. [6] - Boosts AR by 20% to all allies within area of effect. Buff power does not increase as weapon is upgraded. [7] - Once the weapon's durability has reached zero, you can still use the R2 to get the "dance" animation but you won't receive the buff.

description: Carved from the bones of fellow demons. Wielded by the slim lesser Batwing demons. The weapon of these chaos demons wandering Anor Londo are different from those of other chaos demons, and are imbued with lightning."
location: [1] - Sold by Shiva of the East for 15,000 souls. [2] - Drops from Bat Wing Demon (2% Chance - Anor Londo).

description: Cross spear born from the soul of Ornstein, a Dragonslayer guarding Anor Londo cathedral.Inflicts lightning damage; effective against dragons.Two-handed thrust relies on cross and buries deep within a dragon's hide, and sends human foes flying."
location: [1] - Ascend +10 spear with Soul of Ornstein.

description: Four-pronged Plow wielded by the wooden scarecrows, serfs of the forest Sanctuary. The scarecrow serfs would not normally use these four-pronged plows as weapons, but their sharpness makes them very deadly."
location: [1] - Drops from the Treant Gardener in the Royal Wood.

description: Weapon born from the mystical creature of the Darkroot Garden, the Moonlight Butterfly. The horns of the butterfly, a being created by Seath, are imbued with a pure magic power."
location: [1] - Ascension: Ascend a +10 Spear or Rapier class weapon with the Soul of the Moonlight Butterfly, at the Giant Blacksmith in Anor Londo.

description: Spear with blade attached to a broad point. Boasts long reach, can slice." "The wide range of this spear makes it adaptable to many situations. Its strength lies in its length, but in cramped quarters, this backfires, and slices ricochet off walls."
location: [1] - Left of the main area in Darkroot Garden (the same area with the Elite Knight Set) sitting on a corpse below a 2-headed Tree-Lizard. [2] - Video Location Timestamp 5:44.

description: Long spear with a very long red hilt. Traditionally used by groups of soldiers. Specially designed for distance thrusting. Has the longest range of the non-projectile weapons, but its very length makes it somewhat difficult to handle."
location: NA

description: The silver knights of Anor Londo guard the city using this beautifully slender weapon. The spear can be wielded by both hands in a focused thrust that uses one's body weight, or swung in a large sweeping motion."
location: [1] - Drops from Silver Knight (2% Chance - Anor Londo).

description: Standard spear used commonly by soldiers. Long reach, and can be used with shield up. Effective against hard exteriors, and hits for high damage at the right moment of an enemy's swing. But the hit radius is small, and it is easily blocked by shields."
location: [1] - Sold By: Male Undead Merchant (600 souls). [2] - Drops from Spear Wielder Infested Ghoul 2% chance - Blighttown). [3] - Dropped by Phalanx and Undead Soldier. [4] - Drops from Phalanx (1% Chance - Painted World of Ariamis). [5] - Drops from Undead Soldier wielding a spear and shield (2% Chance - Undead Burg, Undead Parish, Northern Undead Asylum (Revisit), Firelink Shrine). [6] - A Lightning version drops from a Mimic in the Sen's Fortress.

description: A long-hilted spear with a barbed point. Long reach, and can be used with shield up. Effective against hard exteriors, and hits for high damage at the right moment of an enemy's swing. But the hit radius is small, and it is easily blocked by shields."
location: [1] - Video Location Timestamp 1:35.

description: Straight sword of an unknown knight, likely one of Astora's superiors.""High-quality weapon with a powerful blessing."
location: [1] - Can be found in front of the Undead Dragon in the Valley of Drakes. [2] - Can be acquired very early if you have the Master Key. Go to New Londo Ruins from Firelink Shrine, then head right to the Valley of Drakes and use the Master Key to unlock the gate. This allows you to get to the sword immediately after arriving at Firelink Shrine. The Undead Dragon will wake up (so expect a fight) when the sword is picked up, it is the item on the left out of the two items. [3] - Since the Undead Dragon can one hit kill a low level character, try to be fast and at least pick up the sword before dying. The item on the right out of the two items is the Dragon Crest Shield which is also a good item. You can pick up both items before the Undead Dragon attacks if you are fast enough. You can pick the sword up without waking the dragon but not the shield. [4] - Video Location Timestamp 1:16.

description: The knights of the ancient kingdom of Balder wielded these rock-solid, long swords which are excellent for thrust attacks." "Balder was the home of Knight King Rendal, but the kingdom was reduced to ruins after a widespread outbreak of Undead."
location: NA
